{
  "conversation_id": "8a473413-c34f-4c5b-9b22-c9e71e4f5657",
  "next_message": null,
  "history": [
    {
      "user": {
        "additional_context": "",
        "env_context": {
          "env_state": {
            "operating_system": "macos",
            "current_working_directory": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice",
            "environment_variables": []
          }
        },
        "content": {
          "Prompt": {
            "prompt": "one pain point i have is creating expenses automatically for regular bills as the amounts could change based on usage so i dont want to have a recurring bill kind of thing but is there way we can setup an endpoint created per user (unique to each company) to which we can forward emails to create expenses automatically? or any smart \nway?\n"
          }
        },
        "timestamp": "2026-04-01T16:35:55.740286+13:00",
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "30cda700-4658-4aa6-bbdf-52130083308b",
          "content": "Great idea — this is a common pain point. Let me outline a few approaches, from simplest to most sophisticated, that would work well with your Amplify Gen 2 + Next.js stack:\n\n## Option 1: Unique Email Endpoint per Company (Recommended)\n\nUse **AWS SES (Simple Email Service)** to receive emails on a per-company subdomain/address:\n\n**How it works:**\n1. Each company gets a unique email like `expenses+{companyId}@inbound.cloudproinvoice.com` (or `{companyId}@expenses.cloudproinvoice.com`)\n2. SES receives the email → triggers a Lambda function\n3. Lambda parses the email (sender, subject, body, attachments like PDF invoices)\n4. Optionally use **Bedrock/Claude** to extract vendor, amount, date, line items from the email/attachment\n5. Auto-create an Expense record in DynamoDB, status = `pending_review`\n\n**Architecture:**\n```\nEmail forwarded → SES Inbound → S3 (raw email) → Lambda → Bedrock (extract data) → DynamoDB (Expense)\n                                                                                   → S3 (attachment)\n```\n\n**Pros:** No variable costs per bill, works with any vendor, users just set up an email forwarding rule once.\n\n## Option 2: Unique Webhook URL per Company\n\nEach company gets a URL like `https://api.cloudproinvoice.com/ingest/{apiKey}` where they can:\n- Forward emails via Zapier/Make/Power Automate\n- Connect directly from accounting tools\n- POST receipt images or PDFs\n\nSimpler to implement but requires the user to set up an automation tool as middleware.\n\n## Option 3: Hybrid (Email + AI Extraction)\n\nCombine Option 1 with smart extraction:\n- Forward any bill/receipt email to your unique address\n- Use Bedrock Claude to parse the email + any PDF/image attachments\n- Auto-categorize (utilities, software, office supplies, etc.)\n- Create a draft expense the user just confirms with one click\n\n---\n\n## My Recommendation\n\nOption 1 (SES inbound email) with AI extraction is the most seamless UX. The user sets up one forwarding rule per vendor in their email client and never thinks about it again.\n\nWant me to start building this out? I'd approach it as:\n\n1. Add an `Expense` model + `CompanyIngestEmail` to your Amplify data schema\n2. Create the SES inbound email receiving infrastructure (this would be a custom CDK construct in your Amplify backend)\n3. Build the Lambda that parses emails and calls Bedrock for extraction\n4. Add a dashboard page to view/approve auto-created expenses\n\nWhich direction appeals to you, or do you want to explore a different angle?"
        }
      },

    {
      "user": {
        "additional_context": "",
        "env_context": {
          "env_state": {
            "operating_system": "macos",
            "current_working_directory": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice",
            "environment_variables": []
          }
        },
        "content": {
          "Prompt": {
            "prompt": "we use texttract currently in our stack i used AI in my legacy app ai cant read pdfs directly can read images Texttract some time does not give accurate details how do we deal this"
          }
        },
        "timestamp": "2026-04-01T16:38:44.005854+13:00",
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "75f9b58f-3a41-4e63-b3b3-0807db4cb4fa",
          "content": "Yeah, this is a real problem — Textract is great for structured forms but struggles with varied invoice layouts, and LLMs can't natively read PDFs. Here's a practical pipeline that gets the best of both:\n\n## The Pipeline: Textract + AI Verification\n\n```\nPDF/Image → Textract (OCR) → Raw text/tables → Bedrock Claude (interpret & extract) → Structured Expense\n```\n\nThe key insight: **use Textract for what it's good at (OCR)** and **use AI for what it's good at (understanding context and messy data)**.\n\n### Why this works better than either alone\n\n| Textract alone | AI alone | Textract + AI |\n|---|---|---|\n| Gets raw text but misinterprets layouts | Can't read PDFs/images directly | Textract extracts text, AI understands it |\n| Struggles with varied invoice formats | Hallucinate numbers it can't see | AI validates against raw OCR output |\n| Returns confidence scores but no context | No confidence scores | You get both |\n\n### Dealing with Textract inaccuracies\n\nA few practical strategies:\n\n1. **Use `AnalyzeExpense` not `AnalyzeDocument`** — Textract has a specific expense/invoice API that understands vendor names, line items, totals, tax, etc. Much better than generic document analysis.\n\n2. **Send both raw text AND tables to the LLM** — give Claude the full Textract output and ask it to reconcile:\n\n```\nYou are extracting expense data from OCR output of an invoice.\nThe OCR may contain errors. Use context clues to correct obvious mistakes \n(e.g., \"Arnount\" → \"Amount\", \"S1,234.56\" → \"$1,234.56\").\n\nExtract: vendor, date, total, tax, currency, line_items[]\n\nIf any field is uncertain, set confidence to \"low\".\n\nOCR Text:\n{textract_raw_text}\n\nOCR Tables:\n{textract_tables}\n```\n\n3. **Confidence-based routing:**\n   - High confidence → auto-create expense as `draft`\n   - Low confidence → flag for manual review, show the original PDF alongside extracted data\n\n4. **For images in emails** (screenshots of receipts etc.) — convert to base64 and send directly to Claude's vision. That path skips Textract entirely since Claude can read images natively.\n\n### Simplified flow\n\n```\nInbound email\n  ├── PDF attachment → Textract AnalyzeExpense → raw text → Bedrock Claude → structured data\n  ├── Image attachment → Bedrock Claude (vision, direct) → structured data\n  └── Email body text → Bedrock Claude (direct) → structured data\n                                                        ↓\n                                                  confidence check\n                                                   ├── high → auto-create draft expense\n                                                   └── low  → queue for review\n```\n\nThis way you're using the right tool for each input type, and the AI acts as the \"smart layer\" that handles the messiness Textract leaves behind.\n\nWant me to start scaffolding this out in your Amplify backend — the Lambda handler + Bedrock prompt + Textract call?"
        }
      },

      "user": {
        "additional_context": "",
        "env_context": {
          "env_state": {
            "operating_system": "macos",

        },
        "content": {
          "Prompt": {
            "prompt": "sunds good but the cost we going to incur "
          }
        },
        "timestamp": "2026-04-01T16:41:06.766662+13:00",
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "b91b8e8e-b3ec-441b-a17d-5f7a8346a7bf",
          "content": "Valid concern. Let's break down the actual costs per invoice processed:\n\n## Cost Per Invoice (ap-southeast-2 pricing)\n\n| Service | Per Invoice | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| SES Inbound | ~$0.00 | First 1,000/month free, then $0.10/1,000 |\n| S3 (store email + PDF) | ~$0.0001 | Negligible |\n| Textract AnalyzeExpense | **$0.01** | Per page |\n| Bedrock Claude Sonnet | ~$0.01-0.02 | ~1K input + 500 output tokens |\n| Lambda | ~$0.0001 | Negligible |\n| DynamoDB | ~$0.0001 | Negligible |\n\n**Total: ~$0.02–0.03 per invoice processed**\n\n## Monthly Cost Estimates\n\n| Invoices/month | Cost |\n|---|---|\n| 50 (small business) | ~$1.50 |\n| 200 (medium) | ~$6.00 |\n| 1,000 (larger) | ~$30.00 |\n\n## Ways to Cut Costs Further\n\n1. **Skip Textract for image attachments** — send directly to Claude vision. Saves $0.01/invoice for images (receipts, screenshots).\n\n2. **Use Claude Haiku instead of Sonnet** for extraction — it's ~10x cheaper and honestly good enough for structured extraction tasks. Drops AI cost to ~$0.001/invoice.\n\n3. **Cache known vendors** — if you've seen a Spark Energy bill before, you already know the layout. Skip AI for repeat formats and just use Textract + a template.\n\n4. **Only call Textract on PDFs** — email body text goes straight to AI, no OCR needed.\n\n## Optimised Cost\n\n| Invoices/month | With Haiku + smart routing |\n|---|---|\n| 50 | ~$0.50 |\n| 200 | ~$2.00 |\n| 1,000 | ~$12.00 |\n\nFor context, most expense management tools charge $5-15/user/month. This feature could actually be a selling point for CloudPro at a fraction of the cost.\n\nThe real question is — do you want to absorb this as a platform cost, or make it a premium feature for paid tiers? Either way the margins are very healthy.\n\nWant to move forward with the implementation?"
        }
      },

              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_MvOdXG1JvctY1NuiVkGWRw",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "=== Operation 1 Result (Text) ===\nimport { type ClientSchema, a, defineData } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\nimport { sendInvoiceEmail } from '../functions/send-invoice-email/resource';\nimport { processReceipt } from '../functions/process-receipt/resource';\n\nconst schema = a.schema({\n  // Company Profile - stores user's business details\n  CompanyProfile: a\n    .model({\n      companyName: a.string().required(),\n      companyEmail: a.string(),\n      companyPhone: a.string(),\n      companyAddress: a.string(),\n      companyCity: a.string(),\n      companyState: a.string(),\n      companyPostalCode: a.string(),\n      companyCountry: a.string().default('New Zealand'),\n      gstNumber: a.string(), // NZ GST number\n      bankAccount: a.string(), // NZ bank account\n      defaultCurrency: a.string().default('NZD'),\n      defaultGstRate: a.float().default(15), // NZ GST is 15%\n      logoUrl: a.string(),\n      // Email preferences\n      emailSubjectTemplate: a.string().default('Invoice {invoiceNumber} from {companyName}'),\n      emailBodyTemplate: a.string().default('Please find attached invoice {invoiceNumber} for {total}. Payment is due by {dueDate}. Thank you for your business.'),\n      emailReplyTo: a.string(),\n      emailCcSelf: a.boolean().default(true),\n      defaultTemplate: a.string().default('modern'),\n      // Reminder settings\n      reminderEnabled: a.boolean().default(false),\n      reminderDaysBefore: a.string().default('7,3,1'),\n      reminderDaysAfter: a.string().default('1,7,14'),\n      reminderSubjectTemplate: a.string().default('Reminder: Invoice {invoiceNumber} from {companyName}'),\n      reminderBodyTemplate: a.string().default('This is a friendly reminder that invoice {invoiceNumber} for {total} is due on {dueDate}. Please arrange payment at your earliest convenience.'),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),\n\n  User: a\n    .model({\n      email: a.string().required(),\n      firstName: a.string(),\n      lastName: a.string(),\n      companyProfile: a.hasOne('CompanyProfile', 'userId'),\n      invoices: a.hasMany('Invoice', 'userId'),\n      clients: a.hasMany('Client', 'userId'),\n      expenses: a.hasMany('Expense', 'userId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),\n\n  Client: a\n    .model({\n      name: a.string().required(),\n      email: a.string(),\n      phone: a.string(),\n      address: a.string(),\n      city: a.string(),\n      state: a.string(),\n      postalCode: a.string(),\n      country: a.string().default('New Zealand'),\n      notes: a.string(),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n      invoices: a.hasMany('Invoice', 'clientId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),\n\n  Invoice: a\n    .model({\n      invoiceNumber: a.string().required(),\n      clientName: a.string().required(),\n      clientEmail: a.string(),\n      clientAddress: a.string(),\n      issueDate: a.datetime().required(),\n      dueDate: a.datetime(),\n      items: a.hasMany('InvoiceItem', 'invoiceId'),\n      notes: a.string(),\n      paymentTerms: a.string().default('Due by 20th of the month'),\n      subtotal: a.float().required(),\n      gstRate: a.float().default(15), // NZ GST rate\n      gstAmount: a.float(),\n      total: a.float().required(),\n      currency: a.string().default('NZD'),\n      status: a.enum(['DRAFT', 'SENT', 'PAID', 'OVERDUE', 'CANCELLED']),\n      pdfUrl: a.string(),\n      portalToken: a.string(),\n      lastReminderSent: a.datetime(),\n      reminderCount: a.integer().default(0),\n      // Company details at time of invoice (snapshot)\n      companyName: a.string(),\n      companyEmail: a.string(),\n      companyPhone: a.string(),\n      companyAddress: a.string(),\n      gstNumber: a.string(),\n      bankAccount: a.string(),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n      clientId: a.string(),\n      client: a.belongsTo('Client', 'clientId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => [allow.owner(), allow.publicApiKey().to(['read'])]),\n\n  InvoiceItem: a\n    .model({\n      description: a.string().required(),\n      wbs: a.string(), // Work Breakdown Structure / Project code\n      quantity: a.float().required(),\n      unitPrice: a.float().required(),\n      amount: a.float().required(),\n      invoiceId: a.string().required(),\n      invoice: a.belongsTo('Invoice', 'invoiceId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => [allow.owner(), allow.publicApiKey().to(['read'])]),\n\n  Expense: a\n    .model({\n      description: a.string().required(),\n      category: a.string(),\n      amount: a.float().required(),\n      amountExGst: a.float(), // Amount excluding GST\n      gstAmount: a.float(), // GST component\n      gstClaimable: a.boolean().default(true), // Can claim GST back\n      date: a.datetime().required(),\n      receiptUrl: a.string(),\n      notes: a.string(),\n      status: a.enum(['PENDING', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED']),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),\n\n  RecurringInvoice: a\n    .model({\n      clientId: a.string().required(),\n      clientName: a.string().required(),\n      clientEmail: a.string(),\n      frequency: a.enum(['WEEKLY', 'FORTNIGHTLY', 'MONTHLY', 'QUARTERLY', 'ANNUALLY']),\n      nextDate: a.date().required(),\n      endDate: a.date(),\n      active: a.boolean().default(true),\n      lineItems: a.json(), // JSON array of {description, wbs, quantity, unitPrice, amount}\n      notes: a.string(),\n      paymentTerms: a.string().default('Due by 20th of the month'),\n      currency: a.string().default('NZD'),\n      generatedCount: a.integer().default(0),\n      lastGeneratedDate: a.date(),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),\n\n  // Custom mutation for sending invoice emails\n  sendInvoiceEmail: a\n    .mutation()\n    .arguments({\n      to: a.string().required(),\n      cc: a.string(),\n      replyTo: a.string(),\n      subject: a.string().required(),\n      body: a.string().required(),\n      pdfBase64: a.string().required(),\n      fileName: a.string().required(),\n      fromName: a.string(),\n    })\n    .returns(a.json())\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.authenticated())\n    .handler(a.handler.function(sendInvoiceEmail)),\n\n  processReceipt: a\n    .mutation()\n    .arguments({\n      imageBase64: a.string().required(),\n    })\n    .returns(a.json())\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.authenticated())\n    .handler(a.handler.function(processReceipt)),\n});\n\nexport type Schema = ClientSchema<typeof schema>;\n\nexport const data = defineData({\n  schema,\n  authorizationModes: {\n    defaultAuthorizationMode: 'userPool',\n    apiKeyAuthorizationMode: {\n      expiresInDays: 365,\n    },\n  },\n});\n\n=== Operation 2 Result (Text) ===\nimport { defineBackend } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\nimport { auth } from './auth/resource';\nimport { data } from './data/resource';\nimport { storage } from './storage/resource';\nimport { sendInvoiceEmail } from './functions/send-invoice-email/resource';\nimport { processReceipt } from './functions/process-receipt/resource';\nimport { Effect, PolicyStatement } from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-iam';\nimport { Tags } from 'aws-cdk-lib';\n\nconst backend = defineBackend({\n  auth,\n  data,\n  storage,\n  sendInvoiceEmail,\n  processReceipt,\n});\n\n// Tag all resources\nconst stack = backend.createStack('tags');\nTags.of(stack.node.root).add('app', 'cloudpro-invoice');\nTags.of(stack.node.root).add('project', 'cloudpro');\nTags.of(stack.node.root).add('managed-by', 'amplify');\n\n// Grant SES send permissions to the function\nbackend.sendInvoiceEmail.resources.lambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['ses:SendEmail', 'ses:SendRawEmail'],\n    resources: ['arn:aws:ses:ap-southeast-2:*:identity/*'],\n  })\n);\n\n// Grant Textract permissions to the receipt processor\nbackend.processReceipt.resources.lambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['textract:AnalyzeExpense'],\n    resources: ['*'],\n  })\n);\n\nexport { backend };\n\n=== Operation 3 Result (Text) ===\n/**\n * Process Receipt Lambda — uses AWS Textract AnalyzeExpense to extract\n * structured data (total, date, vendor, tax) from receipt images.\n * Called via GraphQL mutation from the expense form.\n */\nimport { TextractClient, AnalyzeExpenseCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-textract';\n\nconst textract = new TextractClient({});\n\ntype ReceiptPayload = {\n  imageBase64: string;\n};\n\ntype ExtractedField = {\n  type: string;\n  value: string;\n  confidence: number;\n};\n\nexport const handler = async (event: { arguments: ReceiptPayload }) => {\n  const { imageBase64 } = event.arguments;\n  const imageBytes = Buffer.from(imageBase64, 'base64');\n\n  const response = await textract.send(new AnalyzeExpenseCommand({\n    Document: { Bytes: imageBytes },\n  }));\n\n  const fields: ExtractedField[] = [];\n  let total = '';\n  let date = '';\n  let vendor = '';\n  let tax = '';\n\n  for (const doc of response.ExpenseDocuments || []) {\n    for (const field of doc.SummaryFields || []) {\n      const type = field.Type?.Text || '';\n      const value = field.ValueDetection?.Text || '';\n      const confidence = field.ValueDetection?.Confidence || 0;\n\n      fields.push({ type, value, confidence });\n\n      // Map common Textract expense fields\n      if (type === 'TOTAL' || type === 'AMOUNT_PAID') {\n        if (!total) total = value;\n      } else if (type === 'INVOICE_RECEIPT_DATE' || type === 'ORDER_DATE') {\n        if (!date) date = value;\n      } else if (type === 'VENDOR_NAME' || type === 'SUPPLIER_NAME') {\n        if (!vendor) vendor = value;\n      } else if (type === 'TAX') {\n        if (!tax) tax = value;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Clean up total — remove currency symbols, commas\n  const cleanTotal = total.replace(/[^0-9.]/g, '');\n\n  return {\n    success: true,\n    extracted: { total: cleanTotal, date, vendor, tax: tax.replace(/[^0-9.]/g, '') },\n    allFields: fields,\n  };\n};\n\n=== Operation 4 Result (Text) ===\nimport { defineFunction } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\n\nexport const processReceipt = defineFunction({\n  name: 'process-receipt',\n  entry: './handler.ts',\n  runtime: 20,\n  timeoutSeconds: 30,\n  memoryMB: 256,\n});\n\n=== Operation 5 Result (Text) ===\nimport { defineStorage } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\n\nexport const storage = defineStorage({\n  name: 'cloudproInvoices',\n  access: (allow) => ({\n    'invoices/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n    'receipts/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n  }),\n});\n\n=== Operation 6 Result (Text) ===\n{\n  \"name\": \"cloudpro-invoice\",\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"private\": true,\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"dev\": \"next dev\",\n    \"build\": \"next build\",\n    \"start\": \"next start\",\n    \"lint\": \"next lint\",\n    \"test\": \"jest\",\n    \"test:watch\": \"jest --watch\",\n    \"test:coverage\": \"jest --coverage\",\n    \"localstack:start\": \"docker-compose up -d\",\n    \"localstack:stop\": \"docker-compose down\",\n    \"localstack:setup\": \"./scripts/setup-localstack.sh\",\n    \"test:integration\": \"USE_LOCALSTACK=true jest --testPathPattern=integration\",\n    \"sandbox\": \"source .env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && npx ampx sandbox\",\n    \"sandbox:bg\": \"./scripts/start-sandbox.sh\"\n  },\n  \"dependencies\": {\n    \"@aws-amplify/backend\": \"^1.21.0\",\n    \"@aws-amplify/backend-cli\": \"^1.8.2\",\n    \"@aws-sdk/client-sesv2\": \"^3.1009.0\",\n    \"@aws-sdk/client-textract\": \"^3.1018.0\",\n    \"@radix-ui/react-slot\": \"^1.2.4\",\n    \"aws-amplify\": \"^6.16.2\",\n    \"class-variance-authority\": \"^0.7.1\",\n    \"clsx\": \"^2.1.1\",\n    \"date-fns\": \"^4.1.0\",\n    \"jspdf\": \"^4.2.0\",\n    \"jspdf-autotable\": \"^5.0.7\",\n    \"lucide-react\": \"^0.577.0\",\n    \"next\": \"14.2.35\",\n    \"react\": \"^18\",\n    \"react-dom\": \"^18\",\n    \"tailwind-merge\": \"^3.5.0\",\n    \"zod\": \"^3.25.17\"\n  },\n  \"devDependencies\": {\n    \"@aws-sdk/client-cognito-identity-provider\": \"^3.1006.0\",\n    \"@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb\": \"^3.1006.0\",\n    \"@aws-sdk/client-s3\": \"^3.1006.0\",\n    \"@testing-library/jest-dom\": \"^6.9.1\",\n    \"@testing-library/react\": \"^16.3.2\",\n    \"@testing-library/user-event\": \"^14.6.1\",\n    \"@types/jest\": \"^30.0.0\",\n    \"@types/node\": \"^20\",\n    \"@types/react\": \"^18\",\n    \"@types/react-dom\": \"^18\",\n    \"aws-cdk-lib\": \"^2.242.0\",\n    \"aws-sdk-client-mock\": \"^4.1.0\",\n    \"constructs\": \"^10.5.1\",\n    \"esbuild\": \"^0.27.3\",\n    \"eslint\": \"^8\",\n    \"eslint-config-next\": \"14.2.35\",\n    \"jest\": \"^30.3.0\",\n    \"jest-environment-jsdom\": \"^30.3.0\",\n    \"localstack\": \"^1.0.0\",\n    \"postcss\": \"^8\",\n    \"tailwindcss\": \"^3.4.1\",\n    \"ts-node\": \"^10.9.2\",\n    \"tsx\": \"^4.21.0\",\n    \"typescript\": \"^5\"\n  }\n}\n\n=== Operation 7 Result (Text) ===\nimport { DynamoDBClient } from '@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb';\nimport { S3Client } from '@aws-sdk/client-s3';\nimport { CognitoIdentityProviderClient } from '@aws-sdk/client-cognito-identity-provider';\n\nconst isLocalStack = process.env.USE_LOCALSTACK === 'true';\n\nconst localStackConfig = {\n  endpoint: 'http://localhost:4566',\n  region: 'us-east-1',\n  credentials: {\n    accessKeyId: 'test',\n    secretAccessKey: 'test',\n  },\n};\n\nexport const getDynamoDBClient = () => {\n  return new DynamoDBClient(isLocalStack ? localStackConfig : {});\n};\n\nexport const getS3Client = () => {\n  return new S3Client(isLocalStack ? localStackConfig : {});\n};\n\nexport const getCognitoClient = () => {\n  return new CognitoIdentityProviderClient(isLocalStack ? localStackConfig : {});\n};"

        "content": {
          "ToolUseResults": {
            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_kBiRVnA6sPqA2r5IlTrOGo",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "=== Operation 1 Result (Text) ===\n'use client';\n\nimport { useState } from 'react';\nimport { generateClient } from 'aws-amplify/data';\nimport type { Schema } from '@/amplify/data/resource';\nimport { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { ArrowLeft, Upload, X, Camera, Loader2 } from 'lucide-react';\nimport Link from 'next/link';\nimport { uploadData } from 'aws-amplify/storage';\nimport AppLayout from '@/components/AppLayout';\nimport { useTheme } from '@/lib/theme-context';\nimport { tc } from '@/lib/theme-classes';\nimport { useToast } from '@/lib/toast-context';\nimport { expenseSchema, validate, FormErrors } from '@/lib/validation';\nimport { getFY, currentFY, isPreviousFYOpen, fyShort } from '@/lib/fy-utils';\n\n// IRD NZ deductible expense categories (IR3/IR4 aligned)\nconst CATEGORIES = [\n  'Advertising & Marketing',\n  'Communication (Phone & Internet)',\n  'Depreciation',\n  'Entertainment (50% deductible)',\n  'General & Administrative',\n  'Insurance',\n  'Interest & Bank Fees',\n  'Legal & Accounting',\n  'Motor Vehicle',\n  'Office Expenses',\n  'Rent & Rates',\n  'Repairs & Maintenance',\n  'Software & Subscriptions',\n  'Subcontractors',\n  'Travel & Accommodation',\n  'Other',\n];\n\nexport default function NewExpensePage() {\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const t = tc(theme);\n  const toast = useToast();\n  const dark = theme === 'dark';\n  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);\n  const [scanning, setScanning] = useState(false);\n  const [errors, setErrors] = useState<FormErrors>({});\n  const [receiptFile, setReceiptFile] = useState<File | null>(null);\n  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({\n    description: '', category: 'Other', amount: '',\n    gstClaimable: true, date: new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0], notes: ''\n  });\n\n=== Operation 2 Result (Text) ===\nimport { SESv2Client, SendEmailCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-sesv2';\n\nconst ses = new SESv2Client({});\n\ntype EmailPayload = {\n  to: string;\n  cc?: string;\n  replyTo?: string;\n  subject: string;\n  body: string;\n  pdfBase64: string;\n  fileName: string;\n  fromName?: string;\n};\n\nexport const handler = async (event: { arguments: EmailPayload }) => {\n  const { to, cc, replyTo, subject, body, pdfBase64, fileName, fromName } = event.arguments;\n\n  const fromEmail = process.env.SES_FROM_EMAIL;\n  const from = fromName ? `${fromName} <${fromEmail}>` : fromEmail;\n  const boundary = `boundary-${Date.now()}`;\n  const rawParts = [\n    `From: ${from}`,\n    `To: ${to}`,\n    ...(cc ? [`Cc: ${cc}`] : []),\n    ...(replyTo ? [`Reply-To: ${replyTo}`] : []),\n    `Subject: ${subject}`,\n    `MIME-Version: 1.0`,\n    `Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=\"${boundary}\"`,\n    ``,\n    `--${boundary}`,\n    `Content-Type: text/plain; charset=UTF-8`,\n    `Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit`,\n    ``,\n    body,\n    ``,\n    `--${boundary}`,\n    `Content-Type: application/pdf; name=\"${fileName}\"`,\n    `Content-Transfer-Encoding: base64`,\n    `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=\"${fileName}\"`,\n    ``,\n    pdfBase64,\n    ``,\n    `--${boundary}--`,\n  ];\n\n  const rawMessage = rawParts.join('\\r\\n');\n\n  await ses.send(new SendEmailCommand({\n    Content: {\n      Raw: { Data: new TextEncoder().encode(rawMessage) },\n    },\n  }));\n\n  return { success: true };\n};"
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "611ebe70-dccd-49c9-8833-d4a1df901a61",
          "content": "Good, I have a clear picture now. Here's the plan:\n\n1. Add `Expense` model fields for email-ingested expenses + `ExpenseIngestConfig` model\n2. Create the `process-expense-email` Lambda function (SES → Textract → Bedrock → DynamoDB)\n3. Wire up SES inbound + S3 + permissions in `backend.ts`\n4. Add an API route for the webhook alternative\n5. Add a settings page for users to see/manage their ingest email\n\nLet me build this out step by step.",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_8LfX7ESnGLhpCscWsTOk7Z",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "create",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/functions/process-expense-email/resource.ts",
                "file_text": "import { defineFunction } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\n\nexport const processExpenseEmail = defineFunction({\n  name: 'process-expense-email',\n  entry: './handler.ts',\n  runtime: 20,\n  timeoutSeconds: 60,\n  memoryMB: 512,\n  environment: {\n    EXPENSE_TABLE_NAME: process.env.EXPENSE_TABLE_NAME || '',\n    INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE_NAME: process.env.INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE_NAME || '',\n    BEDROCK_MODEL_ID: 'anthropic.claude-3-haiku-20240307-v1:0',\n  },\n});\n",
                "summary": "Create the process-expense-email Lambda function definition"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "create",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/functions/process-expense-email/resource.ts",
                "file_text": "import { defineFunction } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\n\nexport const processExpenseEmail = defineFunction({\n  name: 'process-expense-email',\n  entry: './handler.ts',\n  runtime: 20,\n  timeoutSeconds: 60,\n  memoryMB: 512,\n  environment: {\n    EXPENSE_TABLE_NAME: process.env.EXPENSE_TABLE_NAME || '',\n    INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE_NAME: process.env.INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE_NAME || '',\n    BEDROCK_MODEL_ID: 'anthropic.claude-3-haiku-20240307-v1:0',\n  },\n});\n",
                "summary": "Create the process-expense-email Lambda function definition"
              }
            }
          ]
        }

        "images": null

      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "e3d6b053-a276-454e-9a2b-fb6ebe359573",
          "content": "",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_wDK8gHbdM4qUTPlURlysRO",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "create",
                "file_text": "/**\n * Process Expense Email Lambda\n *\n * Triggered by SES inbound email → S3 → this Lambda.\n * Flow: Parse email → extract attachments → Textract (PDF) or Bedrock Vision (images)\n *       → Bedrock Haiku (interpret & structure) → create draft Expense in DynamoDB.\n */\nimport { S3Client, GetObjectCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-s3';\nimport { TextractClient, AnalyzeExpenseCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-textract';\nimport { BedrockRuntimeClient, InvokeModelCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime';\nimport { DynamoDBClient } from '@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb';\nimport { DynamoDBDocumentClient, PutCommand, QueryCommand } from '@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb';\nimport { simpleParser, ParsedMail, Attachment } from 'mailparser';\n\nconst s3 = new S3Client({});\nconst textract = new TextractClient({});\nconst bedrock = new BedrockRuntimeClient({});\nconst ddb = DynamoDBDocumentClient.from(new DynamoDBClient({}));\n\nconst EXPENSE_TABLE = process.env.EXPENSE_TABLE_NAME!;\nconst INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE = process.env.INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE_NAME!;\nconst BEDROCK_MODEL = process.env.BEDROCK_MODEL_ID!;\n\ninterface ExtractedExpense {\n  vendor: string;\n  description: string;\n  total: string;\n  tax: string;\n  date: string;\n  category: string;\n  confidence: 'high' | 'low';\n}\n\n// SES event puts raw email in S3\ninterface SESEvent {\n  Records: Array<{\n    ses: {\n      mail: {\n        messageId: string;\n        destination: string[];\n        source: string;\n      };\n    };\n  }>;\n}\n\nexport const handler = async (event: SESEvent) => {\n  const record = event.Records[0];\n  const { messageId, destination } = record.ses.mail;\n  const recipientEmail = destination[0]?.toLowerCase();\n\n  // Extract ingest key from email address: expenses+{ingestKey}@domain or {ingestKey}@expenses.domain\n  const ingestKey = extractIngestKey(recipientEmail);\n  if (!ingestKey) {\n    console.error('Could not extract ingest key from:', recipientEmail);\n    return { status: 'ignored', reason: 'no_ingest_key' };\n  }\n\n  // Look up which user/owner this ingest key belongs to\n  const config = await lookupIngestConfig(ingestKey);\n  if (!config) {\n    console.error('No ingest config found for key:', ingestKey);\n    return { status: 'ignored', reason: 'unknown_key' };\n  }\n\n  // Fetch raw email from S3 (SES stores it there)\n  const bucket = process.env.SES_BUCKET_NAME!;\n  const key = `inbound-emails/${messageId}`;\n  const raw = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: bucket, Key: key }));\n  const emailBuffer = Buffer.from(await raw.Body!.transformToByteArray());\n\n  // Parse the email\n  const parsed: ParsedMail = await simpleParser(emailBuffer);\n  const results: ExtractedExpense[] = [];\n\n  // Process attachments\n  const attachments = parsed.attachments || [];\n  for (const att of attachments) {\n    if (isPdf(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processPdfWithTextractAndAI(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    } else if (isImage(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processImageWithVision(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // If no attachments, try to extract from email body\n  if (results.length === 0 && parsed.text) {\n    const extracted = await extractFromEmailBody(parsed.text, record.ses.mail.source);\n    if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n  }\n\n  // Create draft expenses\n  for (const expense of results) {\n    await createDraftExpense(expense, config.owner, config.userId);\n  }\n\n  return { status: 'processed', expenses: results.length };\n};\n\nfunction extractIngestKey(email: string): string | null {\n  // Format: expenses+{key}@domain.com\n  const plusMatch = email.match(/^expenses\\+([a-z0-9]+)@/);\n  if (plusMatch) return plusMatch[1];\n  // Format: {key}@expenses.domain.com\n  const subdomainMatch = email.match(/^([a-z0-9]+)@expenses\\./);\n  if (subdomainMatch) return subdomainMatch[1];\n  return null;\n}\n\nasync function lookupIngestConfig(ingestKey: string) {\n  const result = await ddb.send(new QueryCommand({\n    TableName: INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE,\n    IndexName: 'byIngestKey',\n    KeyConditionExpression: 'ingestKey = :key',\n    ExpressionAttributeValues: { ':key': ingestKey },\n    Limit: 1,\n  }));\n  return result.Items?.[0] as { owner: string; userId: string } | undefined;\n}\n\nfunction isPdf(att: Attachment): boolean {\n  return att.contentType === 'application/pdf' || att.filename?.toLowerCase().endsWith('.pdf') || false;\n}\n\nfunction isImage(att: Attachment): boolean {\n  return att.contentType?.startsWith('image/') || false;\n}\n\nasync function processPdfWithTextractAndAI(pdfBytes: Buffer): Promise<ExtractedExpense | null> {\n  // Step 1: Textract AnalyzeExpense for OCR\n  const textractResult = await textract.send(new AnalyzeExpenseCommand({\n    Document: { Bytes: pdfBytes },\n  }));\n\n  // Collect all fields from Textract\n  const fields: Array<{ type: string; value: string; confidence: number }> = [];\n  for (const doc of textractResult.ExpenseDocuments || []) {\n    for (const field of doc.SummaryFields || []) {\n      fields.push({\n        type: field.Type?.Text || '',\n        value: field.ValueDetection?.Text || '',\n        confidence: field.ValueDetection?.Confidence || 0,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Step 2: Send to Bedrock to interpret\n  return await interpretWithAI(JSON.stringify(fields));\n}\n\nasync function processImageWithVision(imageBytes: Buffer): Promise<ExtractedExpense | null> {\n  // Use Claude vision directly for images — skip Textract\n  const base64 = imageBytes.toString('base64');\n  const mediaType = 'image/jpeg'; // works for png too\n\n  const response = await bedrock.send(new InvokeModelCommand({\n    modelId: BEDROCK_MODEL,\n    contentType: 'application/json',\n    body: JSON.stringify({\n      anthropic_version: 'bedrock-2023-05-31',\n      max_tokens: 1024,\n      messages: [{\n        role: 'user',\n        content: [\n          {\n            type: 'image',\n            source: { type: 'base64', media_type: mediaType, data: base64 },\n          },\n          {\n            type: 'text',\n            text: EXTRACTION_PROMPT,\n          },\n        ],\n      }],\n    }),\n  }));\n\n  return parseAIResponse(Buffer.from(response.body).toString());\n}\n\nasync function extractFromEmailBody(bodyText: string, senderEmail: string): Promise<ExtractedExpense | null> {\n  const prompt = `${EXTRACTION_PROMPT}\\n\\nSender: ${senderEmail}\\n\\nEmail body:\\n${bodyText.substring(0, 4000)}`;\n  return await interpretWithAI(prompt);\n}\n\nasync function interpretWithAI(ocrOrText: string): Promise<ExtractedExpense | null> {\n  const response = await bedrock.send(new InvokeModelCommand({\n    modelId: BEDROCK_MODEL,\n    contentType: 'application/json',\n    body: JSON.stringify({\n      anthropic_version: 'bedrock-2023-05-31',\n      max_tokens: 1024,\n      messages: [{\n        role: 'user',\n        content: `${EXTRACTION_PROMPT}\\n\\nOCR/Source data:\\n${ocrOrText.substring(0, 8000)}`,\n      }],\n    }),\n  }));\n\n  return parseAIResponse(Buffer.from(response.body).toString());\n}\n\nconst EXTRACTION_PROMPT = `You are extracting expense data from an invoice, receipt, or bill.\nThe OCR may contain errors. Use context clues to correct obvious mistakes.\n\nReturn ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no explanation):\n{\n  \"vendor\": \"company name\",\n  \"description\": \"brief description of what this expense is for\",\n  \"total\": \"123.45\",\n  \"tax\": \"15.00\",\n  \"date\": \"2026-01-15\",\n  \"category\": \"one of: Advertising & Marketing, Communication (Phone & Internet), Depreciation, Entertainment (50% deductible), General & Administrative, Insurance, Interest & Bank Fees, Legal & Accounting, Motor Vehicle, Office Expenses, Rent & Rates, Repairs & Maintenance, Software & Subscriptions, Subcontractors, Travel & Accommodation, Other\",\n  \"confidence\": \"high or low\"\n}\n\nIf a field cannot be determined, use empty string. Date format must be YYYY-MM-DD.\nTotal should be the final amount including tax. Tax should be the GST/tax component only.`;\n\nfunction parseAIResponse(responseBody: string): ExtractedExpense | null {\n  try {\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(responseBody);\n    const text = parsed.content?.[0]?.text || '';\n    // Extract JSON from the response\n    const jsonMatch = text.match(/\\{[\\s\\S]*\\}/);\n    if (!jsonMatch) return null;\n    return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]) as ExtractedExpense;\n  } catch {\n    console.error('Failed to parse AI response');\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\nasync function createDraftExpense(expense: ExtractedExpense, owner: string, userId: string) {\n  const total = parseFloat(expense.total) || 0;\n  const tax = parseFloat(expense.tax) || 0;\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  const id = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 8)}`;\n\n  await ddb.send(new PutCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    Item: {\n      id,\n      __typename: 'Expense',\n      description: expense.vendor ? `${expense.vendor} - ${expense.description}` : expense.description,\n      category: expense.category || 'Other',\n      amount: total,\n      amountExGst: total - tax,\n      gstAmount: tax,\n      gstClaimable: true,\n      date: expense.date ? new Date(expense.date).toISOString() : now,\n      notes: `Auto-created from email. Confidence: ${expense.confidence}`,\n      status: expense.confidence === 'high' ? 'PENDING' : 'PENDING',\n      source: 'email',\n      sourceConfidence: expense.confidence,\n      userId,\n      owner,\n      createdAt: now,\n      updatedAt: now,\n    },\n  }));\n}\n",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/functions/process-expense-email/handler.ts",
                "summary": "Create the main Lambda handler for processing inbound expense emails"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "create",
                "file_text": "/**\n * Process Expense Email Lambda\n *\n * Triggered by SES inbound email → S3 → this Lambda.\n * Flow: Parse email → extract attachments → Textract (PDF) or Bedrock Vision (images)\n *       → Bedrock Haiku (interpret & structure) → create draft Expense in DynamoDB.\n */\nimport { S3Client, GetObjectCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-s3';\nimport { TextractClient, AnalyzeExpenseCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-textract';\nimport { BedrockRuntimeClient, InvokeModelCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime';\nimport { DynamoDBClient } from '@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb';\nimport { DynamoDBDocumentClient, PutCommand, QueryCommand } from '@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb';\nimport { simpleParser, ParsedMail, Attachment } from 'mailparser';\n\nconst s3 = new S3Client({});\nconst textract = new TextractClient({});\nconst bedrock = new BedrockRuntimeClient({});\nconst ddb = DynamoDBDocumentClient.from(new DynamoDBClient({}));\n\nconst EXPENSE_TABLE = process.env.EXPENSE_TABLE_NAME!;\nconst INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE = process.env.INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE_NAME!;\nconst BEDROCK_MODEL = process.env.BEDROCK_MODEL_ID!;\n\ninterface ExtractedExpense {\n  vendor: string;\n  description: string;\n  total: string;\n  tax: string;\n  date: string;\n  category: string;\n  confidence: 'high' | 'low';\n}\n\n// SES event puts raw email in S3\ninterface SESEvent {\n  Records: Array<{\n    ses: {\n      mail: {\n        messageId: string;\n        destination: string[];\n        source: string;\n      };\n    };\n  }>;\n}\n\nexport const handler = async (event: SESEvent) => {\n  const record = event.Records[0];\n  const { messageId, destination } = record.ses.mail;\n  const recipientEmail = destination[0]?.toLowerCase();\n\n  // Extract ingest key from email address: expenses+{ingestKey}@domain or {ingestKey}@expenses.domain\n  const ingestKey = extractIngestKey(recipientEmail);\n  if (!ingestKey) {\n    console.error('Could not extract ingest key from:', recipientEmail);\n    return { status: 'ignored', reason: 'no_ingest_key' };\n  }\n\n  // Look up which user/owner this ingest key belongs to\n  const config = await lookupIngestConfig(ingestKey);\n  if (!config) {\n    console.error('No ingest config found for key:', ingestKey);\n    return { status: 'ignored', reason: 'unknown_key' };\n  }\n\n  // Fetch raw email from S3 (SES stores it there)\n  const bucket = process.env.SES_BUCKET_NAME!;\n  const key = `inbound-emails/${messageId}`;\n  const raw = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: bucket, Key: key }));\n  const emailBuffer = Buffer.from(await raw.Body!.transformToByteArray());\n\n  // Parse the email\n  const parsed: ParsedMail = await simpleParser(emailBuffer);\n  const results: ExtractedExpense[] = [];\n\n  // Process attachments\n  const attachments = parsed.attachments || [];\n  for (const att of attachments) {\n    if (isPdf(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processPdfWithTextractAndAI(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    } else if (isImage(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processImageWithVision(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // If no attachments, try to extract from email body\n  if (results.length === 0 && parsed.text) {\n    const extracted = await extractFromEmailBody(parsed.text, record.ses.mail.source);\n    if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n  }\n\n  // Create draft expenses\n  for (const expense of results) {\n    await createDraftExpense(expense, config.owner, config.userId);\n  }\n\n  return { status: 'processed', expenses: results.length };\n};\n\nfunction extractIngestKey(email: string): string | null {\n  // Format: expenses+{key}@domain.com\n  const plusMatch = email.match(/^expenses\\+([a-z0-9]+)@/);\n  if (plusMatch) return plusMatch[1];\n  // Format: {key}@expenses.domain.com\n  const subdomainMatch = email.match(/^([a-z0-9]+)@expenses\\./);\n  if (subdomainMatch) return subdomainMatch[1];\n  return null;\n}\n\nasync function lookupIngestConfig(ingestKey: string) {\n  const result = await ddb.send(new QueryCommand({\n    TableName: INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE,\n    IndexName: 'byIngestKey',\n    KeyConditionExpression: 'ingestKey = :key',\n    ExpressionAttributeValues: { ':key': ingestKey },\n    Limit: 1,\n  }));\n  return result.Items?.[0] as { owner: string; userId: string } | undefined;\n}\n\nfunction isPdf(att: Attachment): boolean {\n  return att.contentType === 'application/pdf' || att.filename?.toLowerCase().endsWith('.pdf') || false;\n}\n\nfunction isImage(att: Attachment): boolean {\n  return att.contentType?.startsWith('image/') || false;\n}\n\nasync function processPdfWithTextractAndAI(pdfBytes: Buffer): Promise<ExtractedExpense | null> {\n  // Step 1: Textract AnalyzeExpense for OCR\n  const textractResult = await textract.send(new AnalyzeExpenseCommand({\n    Document: { Bytes: pdfBytes },\n  }));\n\n  // Collect all fields from Textract\n  const fields: Array<{ type: string; value: string; confidence: number }> = [];\n  for (const doc of textractResult.ExpenseDocuments || []) {\n    for (const field of doc.SummaryFields || []) {\n      fields.push({\n        type: field.Type?.Text || '',\n        value: field.ValueDetection?.Text || '',\n        confidence: field.ValueDetection?.Confidence || 0,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Step 2: Send to Bedrock to interpret\n  return await interpretWithAI(JSON.stringify(fields));\n}\n\nasync function processImageWithVision(imageBytes: Buffer): Promise<ExtractedExpense | null> {\n  // Use Claude vision directly for images — skip Textract\n  const base64 = imageBytes.toString('base64');\n  const mediaType = 'image/jpeg'; // works for png too\n\n  const response = await bedrock.send(new InvokeModelCommand({\n    modelId: BEDROCK_MODEL,\n    contentType: 'application/json',\n    body: JSON.stringify({\n      anthropic_version: 'bedrock-2023-05-31',\n      max_tokens: 1024,\n      messages: [{\n        role: 'user',\n        content: [\n          {\n            type: 'image',\n            source: { type: 'base64', media_type: mediaType, data: base64 },\n          },\n          {\n            type: 'text',\n            text: EXTRACTION_PROMPT,\n          },\n        ],\n      }],\n    }),\n  }));\n\n  return parseAIResponse(Buffer.from(response.body).toString());\n}\n\nasync function extractFromEmailBody(bodyText: string, senderEmail: string): Promise<ExtractedExpense | null> {\n  const prompt = `${EXTRACTION_PROMPT}\\n\\nSender: ${senderEmail}\\n\\nEmail body:\\n${bodyText.substring(0, 4000)}`;\n  return await interpretWithAI(prompt);\n}\n\nasync function interpretWithAI(ocrOrText: string): Promise<ExtractedExpense | null> {\n  const response = await bedrock.send(new InvokeModelCommand({\n    modelId: BEDROCK_MODEL,\n    contentType: 'application/json',\n    body: JSON.stringify({\n      anthropic_version: 'bedrock-2023-05-31',\n      max_tokens: 1024,\n      messages: [{\n        role: 'user',\n        content: `${EXTRACTION_PROMPT}\\n\\nOCR/Source data:\\n${ocrOrText.substring(0, 8000)}`,\n      }],\n    }),\n  }));\n\n  return parseAIResponse(Buffer.from(response.body).toString());\n}\n\nconst EXTRACTION_PROMPT = `You are extracting expense data from an invoice, receipt, or bill.\nThe OCR may contain errors. Use context clues to correct obvious mistakes.\n\nReturn ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no explanation):\n{\n  \"vendor\": \"company name\",\n  \"description\": \"brief description of what this expense is for\",\n  \"total\": \"123.45\",\n  \"tax\": \"15.00\",\n  \"date\": \"2026-01-15\",\n  \"category\": \"one of: Advertising & Marketing, Communication (Phone & Internet), Depreciation, Entertainment (50% deductible), General & Administrative, Insurance, Interest & Bank Fees, Legal & Accounting, Motor Vehicle, Office Expenses, Rent & Rates, Repairs & Maintenance, Software & Subscriptions, Subcontractors, Travel & Accommodation, Other\",\n  \"confidence\": \"high or low\"\n}\n\nIf a field cannot be determined, use empty string. Date format must be YYYY-MM-DD.\nTotal should be the final amount including tax. Tax should be the GST/tax component only.`;\n\nfunction parseAIResponse(responseBody: string): ExtractedExpense | null {\n  try {\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(responseBody);\n    const text = parsed.content?.[0]?.text || '';\n    // Extract JSON from the response\n    const jsonMatch = text.match(/\\{[\\s\\S]*\\}/);\n    if (!jsonMatch) return null;\n    return JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]) as ExtractedExpense;\n  } catch {\n    console.error('Failed to parse AI response');\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\nasync function createDraftExpense(expense: ExtractedExpense, owner: string, userId: string) {\n  const total = parseFloat(expense.total) || 0;\n  const tax = parseFloat(expense.tax) || 0;\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  const id = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 8)}`;\n\n  await ddb.send(new PutCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    Item: {\n      id,\n      __typename: 'Expense',\n      description: expense.vendor ? `${expense.vendor} - ${expense.description}` : expense.description,\n      category: expense.category || 'Other',\n      amount: total,\n      amountExGst: total - tax,\n      gstAmount: tax,\n      gstClaimable: true,\n      date: expense.date ? new Date(expense.date).toISOString() : now,\n      notes: `Auto-created from email. Confidence: ${expense.confidence}`,\n      status: expense.confidence === 'high' ? 'PENDING' : 'PENDING',\n      source: 'email',\n      sourceConfidence: expense.confidence,\n      userId,\n      owner,\n      createdAt: now,\n      updatedAt: now,\n    },\n  }));\n}\n",

      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "057e6aaa-470c-4ae9-86ba-54a4d6cf8605",
          "content": "",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_rgCHhN3mTrvF93BH64pINx",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "  Expense: a\n    .model({\n      description: a.string().required(),\n      category: a.string(),\n      amount: a.float().required(),\n      amountExGst: a.float(), // Amount excluding GST\n      gstAmount: a.float(), // GST component\n      gstClaimable: a.boolean().default(true), // Can claim GST back\n      date: a.datetime().required(),\n      receiptUrl: a.string(),\n      notes: a.string(),\n      status: a.enum(['PENDING', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED']),\n      // Email ingest fields\n      source: a.enum(['manual', 'email']),\n      sourceConfidence: a.enum(['high', 'low']),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),\n\n  // Stores each company's unique ingest email key\n  ExpenseIngestConfig: a\n    .model({\n      ingestKey: a.string().required(), // unique key for email address\n      ingestEmail: a.string().required(), // full email address for display\n      active: a.boolean().default(true),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n    })\n    .secondaryIndexes((index) => [index('ingestKey')])\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),",
                "old_str": "  Expense: a\n    .model({\n      description: a.string().required(),\n      category: a.string(),\n      amount: a.float().required(),\n      amountExGst: a.float(), // Amount excluding GST\n      gstAmount: a.float(), // GST component\n      gstClaimable: a.boolean().default(true), // Can claim GST back\n      date: a.datetime().required(),\n      receiptUrl: a.string(),\n      notes: a.string(),\n      status: a.enum(['PENDING', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED']),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/data/resource.ts",
                "summary": "Add source/confidence fields to Expense and new ExpenseIngestConfig model"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "  Expense: a\n    .model({\n      description: a.string().required(),\n      category: a.string(),\n      amount: a.float().required(),\n      amountExGst: a.float(), // Amount excluding GST\n      gstAmount: a.float(), // GST component\n      gstClaimable: a.boolean().default(true), // Can claim GST back\n      date: a.datetime().required(),\n      receiptUrl: a.string(),\n      notes: a.string(),\n      status: a.enum(['PENDING', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED']),\n      // Email ingest fields\n      source: a.enum(['manual', 'email']),\n      sourceConfidence: a.enum(['high', 'low']),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),\n\n  // Stores each company's unique ingest email key\n  ExpenseIngestConfig: a\n    .model({\n      ingestKey: a.string().required(), // unique key for email address\n      ingestEmail: a.string().required(), // full email address for display\n      active: a.boolean().default(true),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n    })\n    .secondaryIndexes((index) => [index('ingestKey')])\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),",
                "old_str": "  Expense: a\n    .model({\n      description: a.string().required(),\n      category: a.string(),\n      amount: a.float().required(),\n      amountExGst: a.float(), // Amount excluding GST\n      gstAmount: a.float(), // GST component\n      gstClaimable: a.boolean().default(true), // Can claim GST back\n      date: a.datetime().required(),\n      receiptUrl: a.string(),\n      notes: a.string(),\n      status: a.enum(['PENDING', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED']),\n      userId: a.string().required(),\n      user: a.belongsTo('User', 'userId'),\n    })\n    .authorization((allow) => allow.owner()),",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/data/resource.ts",
                "summary": "Add source/confidence fields to Expense and new ExpenseIngestConfig model"
              }
            }
          ]
        }

        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "93527d78-8791-4937-b6ff-e2399be6bc8e",
          "content": "Now update `backend.ts` to wire up the new function with all required permissions:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_mcgeEENpuVXNzPdbM4tnso",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "create",
                "file_text": "import { defineBackend } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\nimport { auth } from './auth/resource';\nimport { data } from './data/resource';\nimport { storage } from './storage/resource';\nimport { sendInvoiceEmail } from './functions/send-invoice-email/resource';\nimport { processReceipt } from './functions/process-receipt/resource';\nimport { processExpenseEmail } from './functions/process-expense-email/resource';\nimport { Effect, PolicyStatement } from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-iam';\nimport { Tags } from 'aws-cdk-lib';\nimport * as s3 from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-s3';\nimport * as ses from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-ses';\nimport * as sesActions from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-ses-actions';\nimport { RemovalPolicy } from 'aws-cdk-lib';\n\nconst backend = defineBackend({\n  auth,\n  data,\n  storage,\n  sendInvoiceEmail,\n  processReceipt,\n  processExpenseEmail,\n});\n\n// Tag all resources\nconst stack = backend.createStack('tags');\nTags.of(stack.node.root).add('app', 'cloudpro-invoice');\nTags.of(stack.node.root).add('project', 'cloudpro');\nTags.of(stack.node.root).add('managed-by', 'amplify');\n\n// Grant SES send permissions to the function\nbackend.sendInvoiceEmail.resources.lambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['ses:SendEmail', 'ses:SendRawEmail'],\n    resources: ['arn:aws:ses:ap-southeast-2:*:identity/*'],\n  })\n);\n\n// Grant Textract permissions to the receipt processor\nbackend.processReceipt.resources.lambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['textract:AnalyzeExpense'],\n    resources: ['*'],\n  })\n);\n\n// === Expense Email Ingest Infrastructure ===\nconst emailIngestStack = backend.createStack('expense-email-ingest');\n\n// S3 bucket for storing raw inbound emails\nconst inboundEmailBucket = new s3.Bucket(emailIngestStack, 'InboundEmailBucket', {\n  bucketName: undefined, // auto-generated\n  removalPolicy: RemovalPolicy.RETAIN,\n  lifecycleRules: [{ expiration: { days: 30 } }], // clean up after 30 days\n  encryption: s3.BucketEncryption.S3_MANAGED,\n});\n\n// Get DynamoDB table names from the data resource\nconst expenseTableName = backend.data.resources.tables['Expense'].tableName;\nconst ingestConfigTableName = backend.data.resources.tables['ExpenseIngestConfig'].tableName;\n\n// Grant the Lambda permissions\nconst processEmailLambda = backend.processExpenseEmail.resources.lambda;\n\ninboundEmailBucket.grantRead(processEmailLambda);\n\nprocessEmailLambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['textract:AnalyzeExpense'],\n    resources: ['*'],\n  })\n);\n\nprocessEmailLambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['bedrock:InvokeModel'],\n    resources: ['arn:aws:bedrock:ap-southeast-2::foundation-model/*'],\n  })\n);\n\nprocessEmailLambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['dynamodb:PutItem', 'dynamodb:Query', 'dynamodb:GetItem'],\n    resources: [\n      backend.data.resources.tables['Expense'].tableArn,\n      backend.data.resources.tables['ExpenseIngestConfig'].tableArn,\n      `${backend.data.resources.tables['ExpenseIngestConfig'].tableArn}/index/*`,\n    ],\n  })\n);\n\n// Set environment variables on the Lambda\nprocessEmailLambda.addEnvironment('SES_BUCKET_NAME', inboundEmailBucket.bucketName);\nprocessEmailLambda.addEnvironment('EXPENSE_TABLE_NAME', expenseTableName);\nprocessEmailLambda.addEnvironment('INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE_NAME', ingestConfigTableName);\n\n// SES Receipt Rule — receives emails and stores in S3, then triggers Lambda\n// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudproinvoice.com (or your chosen domain)\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(emailIngestStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {\n  receiptRuleSetName: 'cloudpro-expense-ingest',\n});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudproinvoice.com'], // Update with your domain\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\nexport { backend };\n",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/backend.ts",
                "summary": "Update backend.ts with SES inbound email infrastructure, S3 bucket, and Lambda permissions"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "create",
                "file_text": "import { defineBackend } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\nimport { auth } from './auth/resource';\nimport { data } from './data/resource';\nimport { storage } from './storage/resource';\nimport { sendInvoiceEmail } from './functions/send-invoice-email/resource';\nimport { processReceipt } from './functions/process-receipt/resource';\nimport { processExpenseEmail } from './functions/process-expense-email/resource';\nimport { Effect, PolicyStatement } from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-iam';\nimport { Tags } from 'aws-cdk-lib';\nimport * as s3 from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-s3';\nimport * as ses from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-ses';\nimport * as sesActions from 'aws-cdk-lib/aws-ses-actions';\nimport { RemovalPolicy } from 'aws-cdk-lib';\n\nconst backend = defineBackend({\n  auth,\n  data,\n  storage,\n  sendInvoiceEmail,\n  processReceipt,\n  processExpenseEmail,\n});\n\n// Tag all resources\nconst stack = backend.createStack('tags');\nTags.of(stack.node.root).add('app', 'cloudpro-invoice');\nTags.of(stack.node.root).add('project', 'cloudpro');\nTags.of(stack.node.root).add('managed-by', 'amplify');\n\n// Grant SES send permissions to the function\nbackend.sendInvoiceEmail.resources.lambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['ses:SendEmail', 'ses:SendRawEmail'],\n    resources: ['arn:aws:ses:ap-southeast-2:*:identity/*'],\n  })\n);\n\n// Grant Textract permissions to the receipt processor\nbackend.processReceipt.resources.lambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['textract:AnalyzeExpense'],\n    resources: ['*'],\n  })\n);\n\n// === Expense Email Ingest Infrastructure ===\nconst emailIngestStack = backend.createStack('expense-email-ingest');\n\n// S3 bucket for storing raw inbound emails\nconst inboundEmailBucket = new s3.Bucket(emailIngestStack, 'InboundEmailBucket', {\n  bucketName: undefined, // auto-generated\n  removalPolicy: RemovalPolicy.RETAIN,\n  lifecycleRules: [{ expiration: { days: 30 } }], // clean up after 30 days\n  encryption: s3.BucketEncryption.S3_MANAGED,\n});\n\n// Get DynamoDB table names from the data resource\nconst expenseTableName = backend.data.resources.tables['Expense'].tableName;\nconst ingestConfigTableName = backend.data.resources.tables['ExpenseIngestConfig'].tableName;\n\n// Grant the Lambda permissions\nconst processEmailLambda = backend.processExpenseEmail.resources.lambda;\n\ninboundEmailBucket.grantRead(processEmailLambda);\n\nprocessEmailLambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['textract:AnalyzeExpense'],\n    resources: ['*'],\n  })\n);\n\nprocessEmailLambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['bedrock:InvokeModel'],\n    resources: ['arn:aws:bedrock:ap-southeast-2::foundation-model/*'],\n  })\n);\n\nprocessEmailLambda.addToRolePolicy(\n  new PolicyStatement({\n    effect: Effect.ALLOW,\n    actions: ['dynamodb:PutItem', 'dynamodb:Query', 'dynamodb:GetItem'],\n    resources: [\n      backend.data.resources.tables['Expense'].tableArn,\n      backend.data.resources.tables['ExpenseIngestConfig'].tableArn,\n      `${backend.data.resources.tables['ExpenseIngestConfig'].tableArn}/index/*`,\n    ],\n  })\n);\n\n// Set environment variables on the Lambda\nprocessEmailLambda.addEnvironment('SES_BUCKET_NAME', inboundEmailBucket.bucketName);\nprocessEmailLambda.addEnvironment('EXPENSE_TABLE_NAME', expenseTableName);\nprocessEmailLambda.addEnvironment('INGEST_CONFIG_TABLE_NAME', ingestConfigTableName);\n\n// SES Receipt Rule — receives emails and stores in S3, then triggers Lambda\n// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudproinvoice.com (or your chosen domain)\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(emailIngestStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {\n  receiptRuleSetName: 'cloudpro-expense-ingest',\n});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudproinvoice.com'], // Update with your domain\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\nexport { backend };\n",

        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "71d33ac5-40ff-41ad-8cd1-ff21bd8e68f8",
          "content": "Now add the storage path for inbound emails:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_qPduliMhoAvBBVxcx4bjxT",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "import { defineStorage } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\n\nexport const storage = defineStorage({\n  name: 'cloudproInvoices',\n  access: (allow) => ({\n    'invoices/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n    'receipts/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n    'expense-receipts/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n  }),\n});",
                "old_str": "import { defineStorage } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\n\nexport const storage = defineStorage({\n  name: 'cloudproInvoices',\n  access: (allow) => ({\n    'invoices/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n    'receipts/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n  }),\n});",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/storage/resource.ts",
                "summary": "Add expense-receipts storage path"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "import { defineStorage } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\n\nexport const storage = defineStorage({\n  name: 'cloudproInvoices',\n  access: (allow) => ({\n    'invoices/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n    'receipts/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n    'expense-receipts/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n  }),\n});",
                "old_str": "import { defineStorage } from '@aws-amplify/backend';\n\nexport const storage = defineStorage({\n  name: 'cloudproInvoices',\n  access: (allow) => ({\n    'invoices/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n    'receipts/{entity_id}/*': [\n      allow.entity('identity').to(['read', 'write', 'delete']),\n    ],\n  }),\n});",

                    "Json": {

                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "'use client';\n\nimport { useState, useEffect } from 'react';\nimport { useAuth } from '@/lib/auth-context';\nimport { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { generateClient } from 'aws-amplify/data';\nimport { uploadData, getUrl } from 'aws-amplify/storage';\nimport type { Schema } from '@/amplify/data/resource';\nimport { useTheme } from '@/lib/theme-context';\nimport { tc } from '@/lib/theme-classes';\nimport { useToast } from '@/lib/toast-context';\nimport { TEMPLATES } from '@/lib/generate-pdf';\nimport type { TemplateName } from '@/lib/generate-pdf';\n\nconst client = generateClient<Schema>();\n\nexport default function CompanyProfilePage() {\n  const { user, loading: authLoading } = useAuth();\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const t = tc(theme);\n  const toast = useToast();\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);\n  const [logoFile, setLogoFile] = useState<File | null>(null);\n  const [logoPreview, setLogoPreview] = useState<string>('');\n  const [profile, setProfile] = useState({\n    companyName: '', companyEmail: '', companyPhone: '', companyAddress: '',\n    companyCity: '', companyState: '', companyPostalCode: '', companyCountry: 'New Zealand',\n    gstNumber: '', bankAccount: '', defaultCurrency: 'NZD', defaultGstRate: 15,"
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },

            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_FtEDBrFLjE4GyjVqzGQpgT",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "'use client';\n\nimport { useAuth } from '@/lib/auth-context';\nimport { useRouter, usePathname } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { useEffect } from 'react';\nimport Link from 'next/link';\nimport AppLayout from '@/components/AppLayout';\nimport { useTheme } from '@/lib/theme-context';\n\nexport default function SettingsLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\n  const { user, loading } = useAuth();\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const pathname = usePathname();\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const dark = theme === 'dark';\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!loading && !user) router.push('/auth/login');\n  }, [user, loading, router]);\n\n  if (loading) {\n    return <AppLayout><div className=\"min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center\">\n      <div className={dark ? 'text-slate-400' : 'text-gray-600'}>Loading...</div>\n    </div></AppLayout>;\n  }\n  if (!user) return null;\n\n  const tabs = [\n    { name: 'Profile', href: '/settings/profile' },\n    { name: 'Company', href: '/settings/company' },\n    { name: 'Email', href: '/settings/email' },\n    { name: 'Security', href: '/settings/security' },\n  ];\n\n  return (\n    <AppLayout>\n      <main className=\"max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 py-8\">\n        <div className=\"mb-8\">\n          <h2 className={dark ? 'text-3xl font-bold text-white' : 'text-3xl font-bold text-gray-900'}>Settings</h2>\n        </div>\n\n        <div className={`border-b mb-8 ${dark ? 'border-purple-500/30' : 'border-gray-200'}`}>\n          <nav className=\"-mb-px flex space-x-8\">\n            {tabs.map((tab) => (\n              <Link key={tab.name} href={tab.href}\n                className={`whitespace-nowrap py-4 px-1 border-b-2 font-medium text-sm ${\n                  pathname === tab.href\n                    ? dark ? 'border-purple-500 text-purple-400' : 'border-indigo-500 text-indigo-600'\n                    : dark ? 'border-transparent text-slate-400 hover:text-white hover:border-purple-500/50' : 'border-transparent text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 hover:border-gray-300'\n                }`}>\n                {tab.name}\n              </Link>\n            ))}\n          </nav>\n        </div>\n\n        {children}\n      </main>\n    </AppLayout>\n  );\n}"
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
