          "message_id": "6fed92be-da45-4ffd-b61e-c970ac1f1f02",
          "content": "The GSI `byExpenseIngestKey` doesn't exist on the DynamoDB table. This is because Amplify Gen 2 names GSIs differently. Let me check the actual index name:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_vX9j12cVIKEwqLV3FP7tNU",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws dynamodb describe-table \\\n  --table-name \"$(aws dynamodb list-tables --query \"TableNames[?contains(@, 'CompanyProfile')]\" --output text)\" \\\n  --query 'Table.GlobalSecondaryIndexes[].IndexName' 2>&1",
                "summary": "Get the actual GSI name on the CompanyProfile table"
              },

          "content": "Found it. The actual GSI name is `companyProfilesByExpenseIngestKey`, not `byExpenseIngestKey`. And it only exists on the sandbox table — the main branch table doesn't have it yet (that deploy probably failed earlier).\n\nLet me fix the Lambda handler:",

      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "3c4170a8-774c-4b23-82a9-eca96be84305",
          "content": "Pushed. The issue was Amplify Gen 2 names GSIs as `{modelName}sBy{FieldName}` (i.e. `companyProfilesByExpenseIngestKey`) not `byExpenseIngestKey`. \n\nOnce main deploys, send another test email. Note the main branch table also needs the GSI deployed — that should happen with this build. Want me to also fast-forward prod with these fixes?"
        }
      },

      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "2aa18189-9920-4947-8149-16aafede0dc4",
          "content": "Progress — the GSI fix worked! It got past the lookup. Now it's failing on Bedrock — `claude-3-haiku` is marked as legacy. Need to switch to the newer model. Let me check what's available:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_fqz56Omvxa54T5zvxq8dmI",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws bedrock list-foundation-models --query \"modelSummaries[?contains(modelId,'haiku')].{id:modelId,name:modelName,status:modelLifecycle.status}\" --output table 2>&1",
                "summary": "List available Haiku models in Bedrock"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws bedrock list-foundation-models --query \"modelSummaries[?contains(modelId,'haiku')].{id:modelId,name:modelName,status:modelLifecycle.status}\" --output table 2>&1",
                "summary": "List available Haiku models in Bedrock"
              }
            }
          ]
        }

          "content": "Newer Bedrock models require an inference profile instead of direct model ID. Let me find the right one:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_hYIKmxhX30P5crtz4UE3f9",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws bedrock list-inference-profiles --query \"inferenceProfileSummaries[?contains(inferenceProfileId,'haiku')].{id:inferenceProfileId,name:inferenceProfileName,status:status}\" --output table 2>&1",
                "summary": "List available Haiku inference profiles"
              },
              "orig_args": {

          "content": "Getting closer — it's hitting `ap-southeast-4` (Melbourne) for the foundation model. The IAM policy only allows `ap-southeast-2`. The AU inference profile routes to `ap-southeast-4` under the hood. Let me widen the IAM policy:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_IcB45G2x6OHlkGCfRTd6sy",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "    actions: ['bedrock:InvokeModel'],\n    resources: ['*'],",
                "old_str": "    actions: ['bedrock:InvokeModel'],\n    resources: [\n      'arn:aws:bedrock:ap-southeast-2::foundation-model/*',\n      'arn:aws:bedrock:*:*:inference-profile/*',\n    ],",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/backend.ts",
                "summary": "Allow bedrock:InvokeModel on all resources - inference profiles route cross-region"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "    actions: ['bedrock:InvokeModel'],\n    resources: ['*'],",
                "old_str": "    actions: ['bedrock:InvokeModel'],\n    resources: [\n      'arn:aws:bedrock:ap-southeast-2::foundation-model/*',\n      'arn:aws:bedrock:*:*:inference-profile/*',\n    ],",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/backend.ts",
                "summary": "Allow bedrock:InvokeModel on all resources - inference profiles route cross-region"
              }
            }
          ]
        }

      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "8d000fc1-94c3-499b-a688-1e3062e5e47d",
          "content": "Pushed. The AU inference profile routes the actual model call to `ap-southeast-4` (Melbourne), so the region-scoped IAM policy was blocking it. Wildcard resource fixes that.\n\nSend another test once this deploys — we're past SES, S3, Lambda, DynamoDB lookup, and whitelist. Just this IAM permission left."
        }
      },

          "message_id": "9ebf48bf-7a8d-409f-8812-376e5626080c",
          "content": "The problem is clear: the SES receipt rule set `cloudpro-expense-ingest` is a **global resource** (not per-stack) and it already exists from the main branch deploy. Prod is trying to create the same named rule set and failing.\n\nThe fix is to use branch-specific names:",

      "user": {

        "content": {
          "ToolUseResults": {
            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_6mz3WRj4gLTcaiOkhHR9wY",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "    ],\n  })\n);\n\n// Set environment variables on the Lambda\nprocessEmailFn.addEnvironment('SES_BUCKET_NAME', inboundEmailBucket.bucketName);\nprocessEmailFn.addEnvironment('EXPENSE_TABLE_NAME', expenseTableName);\nprocessEmailFn.addEnvironment('COMPANY_PROFILE_TABLE_NAME', companyProfileTableName);\n\n// SES Receipt Rule — receives emails and stores in S3, then triggers Lambda\n// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {\n  receiptRuleSetName: 'cloudpro-expense-ingest',\n});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,"
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "02cca4b9-3d7b-46e3-8584-6b4d795b7ad5",
          "content": "SES only allows **one active receipt rule set** per account anyway. So we shouldn't create a new one per branch — we should only create it once and share it across environments. The simplest fix: remove the hardcoded name so CloudFormation auto-generates a unique one per stack:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_1dczlFHUSJ3LkW1im0BXqo",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "const ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {});",
                "old_str": "const ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {\n  receiptRuleSetName: 'cloudpro-expense-ingest',\n});",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/backend.ts",

      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "463a454e-93af-4264-89a1-13467518cd7e",
          "content": "But wait — this will also recreate the main branch rule set with a new name, which means we'll need to re-activate it. And SES only allows one active rule set. For prod to work, we'll need to switch the active rule set after deploy.\n\nLet me also check — the main branch rule set needs to be deleted first since it has the hardcoded name:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_55vd6Q3mOUdFGLr3GzqTYe",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "cd /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice && git add -u && git commit -m \"fix: remove hardcoded SES rule set name to avoid cross-branch conflict\" && git push 2>&1 | tail -3",
                "summary": "Commit and push"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "cd /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice && git add -u && git commit -m \"fix: remove hardcoded SES rule set name to avoid cross-branch conflict\" && git push 2>&1 | tail -3",
                "summary": "Commit and push"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      },

                    "Json": {
                      "exit_status": "0",
                      "stdout": "[main 040d64c] fix: remove hardcoded SES rule set name to avoid cross-branch conflict\n 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 3 deletions(-)\nTo https://github.com/DonthineniSagar/cloudpro-invoice.git\n   ec9794a..040d64c  main -> main\n",

      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "1fb3892e-bc67-4736-9d39-1adc713b6c1e",
          "content": "Important note: after both main and prod deploy, you'll have two rule sets but **only one can be active at a time**. Since prod is your live environment, you'll want to:\n\n1. Wait for main to redeploy (it'll recreate its rule set with an auto-generated name)\n2. Merge to prod and let it deploy\n3. Activate the **prod** rule set: `aws ses set-active-receipt-rule-set --rule-set-name <prod-rule-set-name>`\n\nFor now, the main deploy will handle the test. Once you're happy with testing, we merge to prod and switch the active rule set. Sound good?"
        }
      },

        "content": {
          "Prompt": {
            "prompt": "do we have to activate rules manually?"
          }
        },
        "timestamp": "2026-04-01T19:46:39.721815+13:00",
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "a7148343-7d0f-404e-9591-8ce3591e4a7e",
          "content": "\n\nUnfortunately yes — SES only allows **one active receipt rule set per account**, and there's no CloudFormation/CDK property to set it as active. It's a manual step (or a custom resource).\n\nBut we can automate it with a CDK custom resource that activates the rule set on deploy. Let me add that:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_OaY1UZN4bIsFDz9y4KO9In",
              "name": "fs_read",
              "orig_name": "fs_read",

    {
      "user": {
        "additional_context": "",
        "env_context": {
          "env_state": {
            "operating_system": "macos",
            "current_working_directory": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice",
            "environment_variables": []
          }
        },
        "content": {
          "ToolUseResults": {
            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_OaY1UZN4bIsFDz9y4KO9In",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\nexport { backend };"
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "5cef543f-af27-4905-9cc0-70f92e3e32c2",
          "content": "",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_0fJ3SKAJWHzVXrVfkeLLo0",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {\n  dropSpam: true,\n});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\n// Auto-activate the SES receipt rule set on deploy\nimport * as cr from 'aws-cdk-lib/custom-resources';\n\nnew cr.AwsCustomResource(dataStack, 'ActivateSesRuleSet', {\n  onCreate: {\n    service: 'SES',\n    action: 'setActiveReceiptRuleSet',\n    parameters: { RuleSetName: ruleSet.receiptRuleSetName },\n    physicalResourceId: cr.PhysicalResourceId.of('activate-ses-ruleset'),\n  },\n  onUpdate: {\n    service: 'SES',\n    action: 'setActiveReceiptRuleSet',\n    parameters: { RuleSetName: ruleSet.receiptRuleSetName },\n    physicalResourceId: cr.PhysicalResourceId.of('activate-ses-ruleset'),\n  },\n  policy: cr.AwsCustomResourcePolicy.fromStatements([\n    new PolicyStatement({\n      actions: ['ses:SetActiveReceiptRuleSet'],\n      resources: ['*'],\n    }),\n  ]),\n});\n\nexport { backend };",
                "old_str": "// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\nexport { backend };",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/backend.ts",
                "summary": "Auto-activate SES receipt rule set on deploy using CDK custom resource"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {\n  dropSpam: true,\n});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\n// Auto-activate the SES receipt rule set on deploy\nimport * as cr from 'aws-cdk-lib/custom-resources';\n\nnew cr.AwsCustomResource(dataStack, 'ActivateSesRuleSet', {\n  onCreate: {\n    service: 'SES',\n    action: 'setActiveReceiptRuleSet',\n    parameters: { RuleSetName: ruleSet.receiptRuleSetName },\n    physicalResourceId: cr.PhysicalResourceId.of('activate-ses-ruleset'),\n  },\n  onUpdate: {\n    service: 'SES',\n    action: 'setActiveReceiptRuleSet',\n    parameters: { RuleSetName: ruleSet.receiptRuleSetName },\n    physicalResourceId: cr.PhysicalResourceId.of('activate-ses-ruleset'),\n  },\n  policy: cr.AwsCustomResourcePolicy.fromStatements([\n    new PolicyStatement({\n      actions: ['ses:SetActiveReceiptRuleSet'],\n      resources: ['*'],\n    }),\n  ]),\n});\n\nexport { backend };",
                "old_str": "// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\nexport { backend };",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/backend.ts",
                "summary": "Auto-activate SES receipt rule set on deploy using CDK custom resource"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      },

                      "stdout": "[main cfe624c] feat: auto-activate SES receipt rule set on deploy via custom resource\n 1 file changed, 27 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)\nTo https://github.com/DonthineniSagar/cloudpro-invoice.git\n   040d64c..cfe624c  main -> main\n",

      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "61220488-5a4e-463c-b913-371b146269c2",
          "content": "Now it's fully automated — no manual steps. On every deploy, the custom resource calls `ses:SetActiveReceiptRuleSet` to activate the rule set for that branch. Also added `dropSpam: true` on the rule set for good measure.\n\nSo when you merge to prod and it deploys, the prod rule set will auto-activate. No more manual console steps."
        }
      },
      "request_metadata": {

    {
      "user": {
        "additional_context": "",
        "env_context": {
          "env_state": {
            "operating_system": "macos",
            "current_working_directory": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice",
            "environment_variables": []
          }
        },
        "content": {
          "Prompt": {
            "prompt": "hmmm that means prod rule will become inactive when i deploy to dev since both are in same account?"
          }
        },
        "timestamp": "2026-04-01T19:53:56.104565+13:00",
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "d0e381c5-9d85-4b71-895f-aa0ebe22d451",
          "content": "\n\nExactly right — since SES only allows one active rule set per account, every main deploy would steal it from prod. Bad idea.\n\nBetter approach: create the rule set **once** outside of Amplify stacks, and have both branches share it. The rule set just routes to the Lambda — and the Lambda already handles the routing per-company via the ingest key.\n\nLet me fix this:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_2sgF9ZEwZVnq5fLhMMsVbf",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\n// SES receipt rule set is created once and shared across branches (only one can be active per account).\n// We import the existing rule set and add a rule pointing to this branch's Lambda + S3 bucket.\nconst ruleSet = ses.ReceiptRuleSet.fromReceiptRuleSetName(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', 'cloudpro-expense-ingest');\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\nexport { backend };",
                "old_str": "// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {\n  dropSpam: true,\n});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\n// Auto-activate the SES receipt rule set on deploy\nimport * as cr from 'aws-cdk-lib/custom-resources';\n\nnew cr.AwsCustomResource(dataStack, 'ActivateSesRuleSet', {\n  onCreate: {\n    service: 'SES',\n    action: 'setActiveReceiptRuleSet',\n    parameters: { RuleSetName: ruleSet.receiptRuleSetName },\n    physicalResourceId: cr.PhysicalResourceId.of('activate-ses-ruleset'),\n  },\n  onUpdate: {\n    service: 'SES',\n    action: 'setActiveReceiptRuleSet',\n    parameters: { RuleSetName: ruleSet.receiptRuleSetName },\n    physicalResourceId: cr.PhysicalResourceId.of('activate-ses-ruleset'),\n  },\n  policy: cr.AwsCustomResourcePolicy.fromStatements([\n    new PolicyStatement({\n      actions: ['ses:SetActiveReceiptRuleSet'],\n      resources: ['*'],\n    }),\n  ]),\n});\n\nexport { backend };",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/backend.ts",
                "summary": "Import existing shared SES rule set instead of creating per-branch - avoids conflict and active rule set switching"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\n// SES receipt rule set is created once and shared across branches (only one can be active per account).\n// We import the existing rule set and add a rule pointing to this branch's Lambda + S3 bucket.\nconst ruleSet = ses.ReceiptRuleSet.fromReceiptRuleSetName(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', 'cloudpro-expense-ingest');\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\nexport { backend };",
                "old_str": "// NOTE: You must verify your domain in SES and set up MX records before this works.\n// Domain: expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz\nconst ruleSet = new ses.ReceiptRuleSet(dataStack, 'ExpenseEmailRuleSet', {\n  dropSpam: true,\n});\n\nruleSet.addRule('ProcessExpenseEmail', {\n  recipients: ['expenses.cloudpro-digital.co.nz'],\n  actions: [\n    new sesActions.S3({\n      bucket: inboundEmailBucket,\n      objectKeyPrefix: 'inbound-emails/',\n    }),\n    new sesActions.Lambda({\n      function: processEmailLambda,\n      invocationType: sesActions.LambdaInvocationType.EVENT,\n    }),\n  ],\n});\n\n// Auto-activate the SES receipt rule set on deploy\nimport * as cr from 'aws-cdk-lib/custom-resources';\n\nnew cr.AwsCustomResource(dataStack, 'ActivateSesRuleSet', {\n  onCreate: {\n    service: 'SES',\n    action: 'setActiveReceiptRuleSet',\n    parameters: { RuleSetName: ruleSet.receiptRuleSetName },\n    physicalResourceId: cr.PhysicalResourceId.of('activate-ses-ruleset'),\n  },\n  onUpdate: {\n    service: 'SES',\n    action: 'setActiveReceiptRuleSet',\n    parameters: { RuleSetName: ruleSet.receiptRuleSetName },\n    physicalResourceId: cr.PhysicalResourceId.of('activate-ses-ruleset'),\n  },\n  policy: cr.AwsCustomResourcePolicy.fromStatements([\n    new PolicyStatement({\n      actions: ['ses:SetActiveReceiptRuleSet'],\n      resources: ['*'],\n    }),\n  ]),\n});\n\nexport { backend };",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/backend.ts",
                "summary": "Import existing shared SES rule set instead of creating per-branch - avoids conflict and active rule set switching"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      },

            {
              "id": "tooluse_TYqAKrKjF0lKyAMujQJMiw",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws ses list-receipt-rule-sets 2>&1",
                "summary": "Check existing rule sets"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws ses list-receipt-rule-sets 2>&1",
                "summary": "Check existing rule sets"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      },

          "message_id": "cc64e8be-5974-45c8-97ac-81e146bb672e",
          "content": "Now commit and push:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_6EW0s6RwnQuTjIvYre8m8D",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "cd /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice && git add -u && git commit -m \"fix: import shared SES rule set instead of creating per-branch\n\nSES only allows one active rule set per account. Instead of creating\none per branch (which causes conflicts), we import the shared\n'cloudpro-expense-ingest' rule set and add rules to it.\nThis is a one-time setup outside of Amplify.\" && git push 2>&1 | tail -3",
                "summary": "Commit and push"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "cd /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice && git add -u && git commit -m \"fix: import shared SES rule set instead of creating per-branch\n\nSES only allows one active rule set per account. Instead of creating\none per branch (which causes conflicts), we import the shared\n'cloudpro-expense-ingest' rule set and add rules to it.\nThis is a one-time setup outside of Amplify.\" && git push 2>&1 | tail -3",
                "summary": "Commit and push"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      },

                      "stdout": "[main 9765794] fix: import shared SES rule set instead of creating per-branch\n 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 27 deletions(-)\nTo https://github.com/DonthineniSagar/cloudpro-invoice.git\n   cfe624c..9765794  main -> main\n",
                      "stderr": ""
                    }
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "2eddbee8-9ae0-4723-bdb0-32bb61bfbcf0",
          "content": "Here's how it works now:\n\n- **One shared SES rule set** (`cloudpro-expense-ingest`) — created once, activated once, lives forever\n- **Each branch deploy** imports it and adds a rule pointing to that branch's Lambda + S3 bucket\n- Deploying main won't break prod, deploying prod won't break main\n- No manual activation needed after the initial setup (which is already done)\n\nBoth branches will have their own rule within the same rule set, each routing to their own Lambda. SES matches on the recipient address and fires all matching rules."
        }
      },

          "message_id": "703ff893-369c-4cd1-86cb-bf721ddca3dc",
          "content": "\n\nYeah, much better. One rule set, both branches add their own rules to it, no conflicts.\n\nOnce the main build finishes and you're happy with testing, let me know and I'll merge everything to prod. We've got a solid stack of fixes since the last prod merge:\n\n- GSI name fix\n- Claude Haiku 4.5 with AU inference profile\n- Bedrock IAM wildcard\n- Shared SES rule set\n- Whitelist loading fix\n- Auto-add pending email on save"
        }
      },

        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "065089ae-52f2-4e75-917b-8300e2140bee",
          "content": "\n\nGood thinking — duplicates are a real problem, especially with email forwarding where people might forward the same bill twice, or manually enter something that was already auto-ingested.\n\nHere are a few approaches, from simplest to smartest:\n\n## 1. Email-level dedup (prevents same email processed twice)\n\nStore the SES `messageId` on the expense. Before processing, check if we've already seen that messageId. This is cheap and catches exact re-forwards.\n\n## 2. Content hash dedup (prevents same attachment processed twice)\n\nHash the PDF/image bytes (SHA-256). If we've seen that exact file before for this user, skip it. Catches the case where someone forwards the same bill from a different email thread.\n\n## 3. Fuzzy matching (catches manual + email duplicates)\n\nBefore creating a draft expense, check if there's already an expense with:\n- Same vendor (fuzzy match)\n- Same amount (exact)\n- Same date (±3 days)\n\nIf all three match → flag as potential duplicate instead of creating a new one.\n\n## My recommendation: combine 1 + 2 + 3\n\n```\nInbound email\n  ├── Check messageId → already processed? → skip\n  ├── Hash attachment → already seen? → skip  \n  └── Extract expense data\n       └── Fuzzy match (vendor + amount + date) against existing expenses\n            ├── Match found → flag as \"possible_duplicate\", link to existing\n            └── No match → create new draft expense\n```\n\nThe key UX decision: **never silently discard** — always create the expense but mark it as a potential duplicate so the user can decide. Show a badge like \"⚠️ Possible duplicate of Spark - $142.50 on 15 Mar\" in the expenses list.\n\n## Cost impact\n\n- MessageId check: free (DynamoDB query)\n- Content hash: free (computed locally)\n- Fuzzy match: one DynamoDB query per expense (negligible)\n- No extra Textract/Bedrock calls\n\nWant me to implement this? I'd add:\n1. `emailMessageId` and `contentHash` fields to `Expense`\n2. Dedup checks in the Lambda before Textract/Bedrock (saves money too — no point processing a duplicate)\n3. Fuzzy match after extraction, before DynamoDB write\n4. `duplicateOf` field linking to the suspected original expense"
        }
      },

        "content": {
          "ToolUseResults": {
            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_QBnVEvxasiMqcfnUGiooCK",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "/**\n * Process Expense Email Lambda\n *\n * Triggered by SES inbound email → S3 → this Lambda.\n * Flow: Parse email → check sender whitelist → extract attachments\n *       → Textract (PDF) or Bedrock Vision (images)\n *       → Bedrock Haiku (interpret & structure) → create draft Expense in DynamoDB.\n */\nimport { S3Client, GetObjectCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-s3';\nimport { TextractClient, AnalyzeExpenseCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-textract';\nimport { BedrockRuntimeClient, InvokeModelCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime';\nimport { DynamoDBClient } from '@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb';\nimport { DynamoDBDocumentClient, PutCommand, QueryCommand } from '@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb';\nimport { simpleParser, ParsedMail, Attachment } from 'mailparser';\n\nconst s3 = new S3Client({});\nconst textract = new TextractClient({});\nconst bedrock = new BedrockRuntimeClient({});\nconst ddb = DynamoDBDocumentClient.from(new DynamoDBClient({}));\n"
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },

    {
      "user": {
        "additional_context": "",
        "env_context": {
          "env_state": {
            "operating_system": "macos",
            "current_working_directory": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice",
            "environment_variables": []
          }
        },
        "content": {
          "ToolUseResults": {
            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_Uz3InsceS8OSqLPDfb4tyG",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "  const sender = senderEmail.toLowerCase().trim();\n  if (!isSenderWhitelisted(sender, config.expenseWhitelistedEmails)) {\n    console.warn(`Sender ${sender} not whitelisted for ingest key ${ingestKey}. Ignoring.`);\n    return { status: 'rejected', reason: 'sender_not_whitelisted', sender };\n  }\n\n  // Fetch raw email from S3\n  const bucket = process.env.SES_BUCKET_NAME!;\n  const key = `inbound-emails/${messageId}`;\n  const raw = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: bucket, Key: key }));\n  const emailBuffer = Buffer.from(await raw.Body!.transformToByteArray());\n\n  const parsed: ParsedMail = await simpleParser(emailBuffer);\n  const results: ExtractedExpense[] = [];\n\n  // Process attachments\n  for (const att of parsed.attachments || []) {\n    if (isPdf(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processPdfWithTextractAndAI(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    } else if (isImage(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processImageWithVision(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    }\n  }\n"
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "f9ac81ff-f24f-4e94-a8fe-03f165189a02",
          "content": "",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_S2NvNIimLD4DY3cBXyePDv",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "  // Fetch raw email from S3\n  const bucket = process.env.SES_BUCKET_NAME!;\n  const key = `inbound-emails/${messageId}`;\n\n  // === DEDUP: Check if this email was already processed ===\n  const existingByMessageId = await findExpenseByField(config.owner, 'emailMessageId', messageId);\n  if (existingByMessageId) {\n    console.log(`Email ${messageId} already processed. Skipping.`);\n    return { status: 'skipped', reason: 'duplicate_email' };\n  }\n\n  const raw = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: bucket, Key: key }));\n  const emailBuffer = Buffer.from(await raw.Body!.transformToByteArray());\n\n  const parsed: ParsedMail = await simpleParser(emailBuffer);\n  const results: Array<{ expense: ExtractedExpense; contentHash: string }> = [];\n\n  // Process attachments\n  for (const att of parsed.attachments || []) {\n    const hash = createHash('sha256').update(att.content).digest('hex');\n\n    // Check if this exact file was already processed\n    const existingByHash = await findExpenseByField(config.owner, 'contentHash', hash);\n    if (existingByHash) {\n      console.log(`Attachment hash ${hash.substring(0, 12)} already processed. Skipping.`);\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    if (isPdf(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processPdfWithTextractAndAI(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push({ expense: extracted, contentHash: hash });\n    } else if (isImage(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processImageWithVision(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push({ expense: extracted, contentHash: hash });\n    }\n  }",
                "old_str": "  // Fetch raw email from S3\n  const bucket = process.env.SES_BUCKET_NAME!;\n  const key = `inbound-emails/${messageId}`;\n  const raw = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: bucket, Key: key }));\n  const emailBuffer = Buffer.from(await raw.Body!.transformToByteArray());\n\n  const parsed: ParsedMail = await simpleParser(emailBuffer);\n  const results: ExtractedExpense[] = [];\n\n  // Process attachments\n  for (const att of parsed.attachments || []) {\n    if (isPdf(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processPdfWithTextractAndAI(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    } else if (isImage(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processImageWithVision(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    }\n  }",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/functions/process-expense-email/handler.ts",
                "summary": "Add messageId dedup check before processing, and content hash dedup per attachment"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "  // Fetch raw email from S3\n  const bucket = process.env.SES_BUCKET_NAME!;\n  const key = `inbound-emails/${messageId}`;\n\n  // === DEDUP: Check if this email was already processed ===\n  const existingByMessageId = await findExpenseByField(config.owner, 'emailMessageId', messageId);\n  if (existingByMessageId) {\n    console.log(`Email ${messageId} already processed. Skipping.`);\n    return { status: 'skipped', reason: 'duplicate_email' };\n  }\n\n  const raw = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: bucket, Key: key }));\n  const emailBuffer = Buffer.from(await raw.Body!.transformToByteArray());\n\n  const parsed: ParsedMail = await simpleParser(emailBuffer);\n  const results: Array<{ expense: ExtractedExpense; contentHash: string }> = [];\n\n  // Process attachments\n  for (const att of parsed.attachments || []) {\n    const hash = createHash('sha256').update(att.content).digest('hex');\n\n    // Check if this exact file was already processed\n    const existingByHash = await findExpenseByField(config.owner, 'contentHash', hash);\n    if (existingByHash) {\n      console.log(`Attachment hash ${hash.substring(0, 12)} already processed. Skipping.`);\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    if (isPdf(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processPdfWithTextractAndAI(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push({ expense: extracted, contentHash: hash });\n    } else if (isImage(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processImageWithVision(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push({ expense: extracted, contentHash: hash });\n    }\n  }",
                "old_str": "  // Fetch raw email from S3\n  const bucket = process.env.SES_BUCKET_NAME!;\n  const key = `inbound-emails/${messageId}`;\n  const raw = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: bucket, Key: key }));\n  const emailBuffer = Buffer.from(await raw.Body!.transformToByteArray());\n\n  const parsed: ParsedMail = await simpleParser(emailBuffer);\n  const results: ExtractedExpense[] = [];\n\n  // Process attachments\n  for (const att of parsed.attachments || []) {\n    if (isPdf(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processPdfWithTextractAndAI(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    } else if (isImage(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processImageWithVision(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n    }\n  }",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/functions/process-expense-email/handler.ts",
                "summary": "Add messageId dedup check before processing, and content hash dedup per attachment"
              }
            }
          ]
        }

                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_j4uEnYdMXznxbxByjWizAb",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "    }\n\n    if (isPdf(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processPdfWithTextractAndAI(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push({ expense: extracted, contentHash: hash });\n    } else if (isImage(att)) {\n      const extracted = await processImageWithVision(att.content);\n      if (extracted) results.push({ expense: extracted, contentHash: hash });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // If no attachments, try email body\n  if (results.length === 0 && parsed.text) {\n    const extracted = await extractFromEmailBody(parsed.text, sender);"
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },

    {
      "user": {
        "additional_context": "",
        "env_context": {
          "env_state": {
            "operating_system": "macos",
            "current_working_directory": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice",

          }
        },
        "content": {
          "ToolUseResults": {
            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_Le3fZt5xmVomRuhBJs97eX",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "\n  // If no attachments, try email body\n  if (results.length === 0 && parsed.text) {\n    const extracted = await extractFromEmailBody(parsed.text, sender);\n    if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n  }\n\n  // Create draft expenses\n  for (const expense of results) {\n    await createDraftExpense(expense, config.owner, config.userId, sender);\n  }\n\n  console.log(`Processed ${results.length} expenses from ${sender} for key ${ingestKey}`);\n  return { status: 'processed', expenses: results.length };\n};\n\n/**\n * Check if sender is in the whitelist.\n * Supports exact email match and wildcard domain match (*@domain.com)."
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "f5b0d586-8895-4547-86c7-da98e06a81cd",
          "content": "",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_lk1pNoe3I7gGljRnO4SG0U",
              "name": "fs_write",
              "orig_name": "fs_write",
              "args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "  // If no attachments, try email body\n  if (results.length === 0 && parsed.text) {\n    const bodyHash = createHash('sha256').update(parsed.text).digest('hex');\n    const extracted = await extractFromEmailBody(parsed.text, sender);\n    if (extracted) results.push({ expense: extracted, contentHash: bodyHash });\n  }\n\n  // Create draft expenses with fuzzy duplicate detection\n  let created = 0;\n  for (const { expense, contentHash } of results) {\n    const duplicate = await findFuzzyDuplicate(config.owner, expense);\n    await createDraftExpense(expense, config.owner, config.userId, sender, messageId, contentHash, duplicate);\n    created++;\n  }\n\n  console.log(`Processed ${created} expenses from ${sender} for key ${ingestKey}`);\n  return { status: 'processed', expenses: created };\n};",
                "old_str": "  // If no attachments, try email body\n  if (results.length === 0 && parsed.text) {\n    const extracted = await extractFromEmailBody(parsed.text, sender);\n    if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n  }\n\n  // Create draft expenses\n  for (const expense of results) {\n    await createDraftExpense(expense, config.owner, config.userId, sender);\n  }\n\n  console.log(`Processed ${results.length} expenses from ${sender} for key ${ingestKey}`);\n  return { status: 'processed', expenses: results.length };\n};",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/functions/process-expense-email/handler.ts",
                "summary": "Add fuzzy duplicate detection before creating expenses, pass dedup fields to createDraftExpense"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "  // If no attachments, try email body\n  if (results.length === 0 && parsed.text) {\n    const bodyHash = createHash('sha256').update(parsed.text).digest('hex');\n    const extracted = await extractFromEmailBody(parsed.text, sender);\n    if (extracted) results.push({ expense: extracted, contentHash: bodyHash });\n  }\n\n  // Create draft expenses with fuzzy duplicate detection\n  let created = 0;\n  for (const { expense, contentHash } of results) {\n    const duplicate = await findFuzzyDuplicate(config.owner, expense);\n    await createDraftExpense(expense, config.owner, config.userId, sender, messageId, contentHash, duplicate);\n    created++;\n  }\n\n  console.log(`Processed ${created} expenses from ${sender} for key ${ingestKey}`);\n  return { status: 'processed', expenses: created };\n};",
                "old_str": "  // If no attachments, try email body\n  if (results.length === 0 && parsed.text) {\n    const extracted = await extractFromEmailBody(parsed.text, sender);\n    if (extracted) results.push(extracted);\n  }\n\n  // Create draft expenses\n  for (const expense of results) {\n    await createDraftExpense(expense, config.owner, config.userId, sender);\n  }\n\n  console.log(`Processed ${results.length} expenses from ${sender} for key ${ingestKey}`);\n  return { status: 'processed', expenses: results.length };\n};",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/functions/process-expense-email/handler.ts",
                "summary": "Add fuzzy duplicate detection before creating expenses, pass dedup fields to createDraftExpense"
              }
            }
          ]
        }

              "args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "async function createDraftExpense(\n  expense: ExtractedExpense, owner: string, userId: string, senderEmail: string,\n  emailMessageId: string, contentHash: string, duplicateOf: string | null\n) {\n  const total = parseFloat(expense.total) || 0;\n  const tax = parseFloat(expense.tax) || 0;\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  const id = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 8)}`;\n  const isDuplicate = !!duplicateOf;\n\n  await ddb.send(new PutCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    Item: {\n      id,\n      __typename: 'Expense',\n      description: expense.vendor ? `${expense.vendor} - ${expense.description}` : expense.description,\n      category: expense.category || 'Other',\n      amount: total,\n      amountExGst: total - tax,\n      gstAmount: tax,\n      gstClaimable: true,\n      ...(expense.date ? { date: new Date(expense.date).toISOString() } : {}),\n      notes: `Auto-created from email (${senderEmail}). Confidence: ${expense.confidence}${!expense.date ? '. Date not found on receipt — please review.' : ''}${isDuplicate ? '. ⚠️ Possible duplicate detected.' : ''}`,\n      status: 'PENDING',\n      source: 'email',\n      sourceConfidence: expense.confidence,\n      emailMessageId,\n      contentHash,\n      suspectedDuplicate: isDuplicate,\n      ...(duplicateOf ? { duplicateOf } : {}),\n      userId,\n      owner,\n      createdAt: now,\n      updatedAt: now,\n    },\n  }));\n}\n\n/** Find an existing expense by a specific field value for this owner */\nasync function findExpenseByField(owner: string, field: string, value: string): Promise<string | null> {\n  const result = await ddb.send(new ScanCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    FilterExpression: '#owner = :owner AND #field = :value',\n    ExpressionAttributeNames: { '#owner': 'owner', '#field': field },\n    ExpressionAttributeValues: { ':owner': owner, ':value': value },\n    Limit: 1,\n    ProjectionExpression: 'id',\n  }));\n  return result.Items?.[0]?.id || null;\n}\n\n/** Fuzzy match: same amount + similar vendor within ±5 days */\nasync function findFuzzyDuplicate(owner: string, expense: ExtractedExpense): Promise<string | null> {\n  const total = parseFloat(expense.total) || 0;\n  if (!total) return null;\n\n  const result = await ddb.send(new ScanCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    FilterExpression: '#owner = :owner AND #amount = :amount',\n    ExpressionAttributeNames: { '#owner': 'owner', '#amount': 'amount' },\n    ExpressionAttributeValues: { ':owner': owner, ':amount': total },\n    ProjectionExpression: 'id, description, #amount, #date',\n    Limit: 10,\n  }));\n\n  if (!result.Items?.length) return null;\n\n  // Check for vendor name similarity + date proximity\n  const expenseDate = expense.date ? new Date(expense.date).getTime() : 0;\n  const vendor = (expense.vendor || '').toLowerCase();\n\n  for (const item of result.Items) {\n    const desc = (item.description || '').toLowerCase();\n    const vendorMatch = vendor && desc.includes(vendor.substring(0, Math.min(vendor.length, 8)));\n\n    if (expenseDate && item.date) {\n      const daysDiff = Math.abs(new Date(item.date).getTime() - expenseDate) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);\n      if (daysDiff <= 5 && vendorMatch) return item.id;\n      if (daysDiff === 0) return item.id; // Same amount + same date = likely duplicate\n    } else if (vendorMatch) {\n      return item.id; // Same amount + same vendor, no dates to compare\n    }\n  }\n\n  return null;\n}",
                "old_str": "async function createDraftExpense(expense: ExtractedExpense, owner: string, userId: string, senderEmail: string) {\n  const total = parseFloat(expense.total) || 0;\n  const tax = parseFloat(expense.tax) || 0;\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  const id = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 8)}`;\n\n  await ddb.send(new PutCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    Item: {\n      id,\n      __typename: 'Expense',\n      description: expense.vendor ? `${expense.vendor} - ${expense.description}` : expense.description,\n      category: expense.category || 'Other',\n      amount: total,\n      amountExGst: total - tax,\n      gstAmount: tax,\n      gstClaimable: true,\n      ...(expense.date ? { date: new Date(expense.date).toISOString() } : {}),\n      notes: `Auto-created from email (${senderEmail}). Confidence: ${expense.confidence}${!expense.date ? '. Date not found on receipt — please review.' : ''}`,\n      status: 'PENDING',\n      source: 'email',\n      sourceConfidence: expense.confidence,\n      userId,\n      owner,\n      createdAt: now,\n      updatedAt: now,\n    },\n  }));\n}",
                "path": "/Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/amplify/functions/process-expense-email/handler.ts",
                "summary": "Add dedup fields to createDraftExpense, add findExpenseByField and findFuzzyDuplicate helpers"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "str_replace",
                "new_str": "async function createDraftExpense(\n  expense: ExtractedExpense, owner: string, userId: string, senderEmail: string,\n  emailMessageId: string, contentHash: string, duplicateOf: string | null\n) {\n  const total = parseFloat(expense.total) || 0;\n  const tax = parseFloat(expense.tax) || 0;\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  const id = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 8)}`;\n  const isDuplicate = !!duplicateOf;\n\n  await ddb.send(new PutCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    Item: {\n      id,\n      __typename: 'Expense',\n      description: expense.vendor ? `${expense.vendor} - ${expense.description}` : expense.description,\n      category: expense.category || 'Other',\n      amount: total,\n      amountExGst: total - tax,\n      gstAmount: tax,\n      gstClaimable: true,\n      ...(expense.date ? { date: new Date(expense.date).toISOString() } : {}),\n      notes: `Auto-created from email (${senderEmail}). Confidence: ${expense.confidence}${!expense.date ? '. Date not found on receipt — please review.' : ''}${isDuplicate ? '. ⚠️ Possible duplicate detected.' : ''}`,\n      status: 'PENDING',\n      source: 'email',\n      sourceConfidence: expense.confidence,\n      emailMessageId,\n      contentHash,\n      suspectedDuplicate: isDuplicate,\n      ...(duplicateOf ? { duplicateOf } : {}),\n      userId,\n      owner,\n      createdAt: now,\n      updatedAt: now,\n    },\n  }));\n}\n\n/** Find an existing expense by a specific field value for this owner */\nasync function findExpenseByField(owner: string, field: string, value: string): Promise<string | null> {\n  const result = await ddb.send(new ScanCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    FilterExpression: '#owner = :owner AND #field = :value',\n    ExpressionAttributeNames: { '#owner': 'owner', '#field': field },\n    ExpressionAttributeValues: { ':owner': owner, ':value': value },\n    Limit: 1,\n    ProjectionExpression: 'id',\n  }));\n  return result.Items?.[0]?.id || null;\n}\n\n/** Fuzzy match: same amount + similar vendor within ±5 days */\nasync function findFuzzyDuplicate(owner: string, expense: ExtractedExpense): Promise<string | null> {\n  const total = parseFloat(expense.total) || 0;\n  if (!total) return null;\n\n  const result = await ddb.send(new ScanCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    FilterExpression: '#owner = :owner AND #amount = :amount',\n    ExpressionAttributeNames: { '#owner': 'owner', '#amount': 'amount' },\n    ExpressionAttributeValues: { ':owner': owner, ':amount': total },\n    ProjectionExpression: 'id, description, #amount, #date',\n    Limit: 10,\n  }));\n\n  if (!result.Items?.length) return null;\n\n  // Check for vendor name similarity + date proximity\n  const expenseDate = expense.date ? new Date(expense.date).getTime() : 0;\n  const vendor = (expense.vendor || '').toLowerCase();\n\n  for (const item of result.Items) {\n    const desc = (item.description || '').toLowerCase();\n    const vendorMatch = vendor && desc.includes(vendor.substring(0, Math.min(vendor.length, 8)));\n\n    if (expenseDate && item.date) {\n      const daysDiff = Math.abs(new Date(item.date).getTime() - expenseDate) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);\n      if (daysDiff <= 5 && vendorMatch) return item.id;\n      if (daysDiff === 0) return item.id; // Same amount + same date = likely duplicate\n    } else if (vendorMatch) {\n      return item.id; // Same amount + same vendor, no dates to compare\n    }\n  }\n\n  return null;\n}",
                "old_str": "async function createDraftExpense(expense: ExtractedExpense, owner: string, userId: string, senderEmail: string) {\n  const total = parseFloat(expense.total) || 0;\n  const tax = parseFloat(expense.tax) || 0;\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  const id = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 8)}`;\n\n  await ddb.send(new PutCommand({\n    TableName: EXPENSE_TABLE,\n    Item: {\n      id,\n      __typename: 'Expense',\n      description: expense.vendor ? `${expense.vendor} - ${expense.description}` : expense.description,\n      category: expense.category || 'Other',\n      amount: total,\n      amountExGst: total - tax,\n      gstAmount: tax,\n      gstClaimable: true,\n      ...(expense.date ? { date: new Date(expense.date).toISOString() } : {}),\n      notes: `Auto-created from email (${senderEmail}). Confidence: ${expense.confidence}${!expense.date ? '. Date not found on receipt — please review.' : ''}`,\n      status: 'PENDING',\n      source: 'email',\n      sourceConfidence: expense.confidence,\n      userId,\n      owner,\n      createdAt: now,\n      updatedAt: now,\n    },\n  }));\n}",

        "content": {
          "ToolUseResults": {
            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_2OrQ3J3uieN5HaFV1xTuaK",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Text": "                    {items.map((expense: any) => {\n                      const needsReceipt = (expense.amount || 0) > 50 && !expense.receiptUrl;\n                      return (\n                        <div key={expense.id}\n                          className={`${dark ? 'bg-black rounded-lg border border-purple-500/30 px-4 py-3' : 'bg-white rounded-lg shadow-sm border border-gray-200 px-4 py-3'}`}>\n                          <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between\">\n                            <Link href={`/expenses/${expense.id}/edit`} className=\"flex items-center gap-3 flex-1 min-w-0\">\n                              {thumbnails[expense.id] ? (\n                                <img src={thumbnails[expense.id]} alt=\"Receipt\"\n                                  onClick={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); setViewingReceipt(thumbnails[expense.id]); }}\n                                  className=\"w-8 h-8 rounded object-cover flex-shrink-0 border border-gray-200 cursor-zoom-in hover:opacity-80\" />\n                              ) : (\n                                <span className=\"text-lg\">{categoryIcon(expense.category || 'Other')}</span>\n                              )}\n                              <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n                                <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2\">\n                                  <p className={`text-sm font-medium truncate ${dark ? 'text-white' : 'text-gray-900'}`}>{expense.description}</p>\n                                  {needsReceipt && <AlertTriangle className=\"w-3 h-3 text-amber-500 flex-shrink-0\" />}\n                                </div>\n                                <p className={`text-xs ${t.textMuted}`}>\n                                  {expense.category || 'Other'} · {expense.date ? new Date(expense.date).toLocaleDateString('en-NZ', { day: 'numeric', month: 'short' }) : 'No date'}\n                                  {expense.gstClaimable && ' · GST'}\n                                </p>\n                              </div>\n                            </Link>\n                            <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 flex-shrink-0 ml-3\">"
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },

                "old_str": "                                  {needsReceipt && <AlertTriangle className=\"w-3 h-3 text-amber-500 flex-shrink-0\" />}",

                "summary": "Create feature branch and commit dedup changes"
              },

        "content": {
          "ToolUseResults": {
            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_w8oE6iSGWPzFUPHB8aVB3n",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Json": {
                      "exit_status": "0",
                      "stdout": "remote:      https://github.com/DonthineniSagar/cloudpro-invoice/pull/new/feature/expense-dedup        \nremote: \nTo https://github.com/DonthineniSagar/cloudpro-invoice.git\n * [new branch]      feature/expense-dedup -> feature/expense-dedup\nbranch 'feature/expense-dedup' set up to track 'origin/feature/expense-dedup'.\n",
                      "stderr": ""
                    }
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "848b6ce3-f54c-4838-bb2a-9939f1bb31c9",
          "content": "",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_Cij6KBcK7j4aAg6QIHPEvu",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "cd /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice && curl -s -X POST \\\n  -H \"Authorization: Bearer $(git credential fill <<< $'protocol=https\\nhost=github.com' 2>/dev/null | grep password | cut -d= -f2)\" \\\n  -H \"Accept: application/vnd.github+json\" \\\n  https://api.github.com/repos/DonthineniSagar/cloudpro-invoice/pulls \\\n  -d '{\n  \"title\": \"feat: Expense Duplicate Detection\",\n  \"head\": \"feature/expense-dedup\",\n  \"base\": \"main\",\n  \"body\": \"## Summary\\n\\nAdds 3-layer duplicate detection for email-ingested expenses.\\n\\n### Layer 1: Email MessageId (exact dedup)\\nSkips processing entirely if the same SES messageId was already processed. Catches re-forwards of the same email.\\n\\n### Layer 2: Content Hash (attachment dedup)\\nSHA-256 hash of each PDF/image attachment. Skips if the same file was already processed. Catches same bill forwarded from different email threads. Saves Textract + Bedrock costs.\\n\\n### Layer 3: Fuzzy Match (cross-source dedup)\\nBefore creating an expense, checks for existing expenses with same amount + similar vendor name within ±5 days. Catches duplicates between manual entry and email ingest.\\n\\n### Behaviour\\n- Layers 1 & 2: silently skip (no cost incurred)\\n- Layer 3: creates the expense but flags it as `suspectedDuplicate: true` with `duplicateOf` linking to the original\\n- UI shows orange \\\"Duplicate?\\\" badge — user decides to keep or delete\\n\\n### Schema Changes\\n- `Expense`: added `emailMessageId`, `contentHash`, `suspectedDuplicate`, `duplicateOf`\"\n}' 2>&1 | grep -E '\"number\"|\"html_url\"' | head -2",
                "summary": "Create PR for dedup feature"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "cd /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice && curl -s -X POST \\\n  -H \"Authorization: Bearer $(git credential fill <<< $'protocol=https\\nhost=github.com' 2>/dev/null | grep password | cut -d= -f2)\" \\\n  -H \"Accept: application/vnd.github+json\" \\\n  https://api.github.com/repos/DonthineniSagar/cloudpro-invoice/pulls \\\n  -d '{\n  \"title\": \"feat: Expense Duplicate Detection\",\n  \"head\": \"feature/expense-dedup\",\n  \"base\": \"main\",\n  \"body\": \"## Summary\\n\\nAdds 3-layer duplicate detection for email-ingested expenses.\\n\\n### Layer 1: Email MessageId (exact dedup)\\nSkips processing entirely if the same SES messageId was already processed. Catches re-forwards of the same email.\\n\\n### Layer 2: Content Hash (attachment dedup)\\nSHA-256 hash of each PDF/image attachment. Skips if the same file was already processed. Catches same bill forwarded from different email threads. Saves Textract + Bedrock costs.\\n\\n### Layer 3: Fuzzy Match (cross-source dedup)\\nBefore creating an expense, checks for existing expenses with same amount + similar vendor name within ±5 days. Catches duplicates between manual entry and email ingest.\\n\\n### Behaviour\\n- Layers 1 & 2: silently skip (no cost incurred)\\n- Layer 3: creates the expense but flags it as `suspectedDuplicate: true` with `duplicateOf` linking to the original\\n- UI shows orange \\\"Duplicate?\\\" badge — user decides to keep or delete\\n\\n### Schema Changes\\n- `Expense`: added `emailMessageId`, `contentHash`, `suspectedDuplicate`, `duplicateOf`\"\n}' 2>&1 | grep -E '\"number\"|\"html_url\"' | head -2",
                "summary": "Create PR for dedup feature"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      },

            "tool_use_results": [
              {
                "tool_use_id": "tooluse_mR06oBOqkQLgMYWjwn0bsh",
                "content": [
                  {
                    "Json": {
                      "exit_status": "0",
                      "stdout": "9765794 fix: import shared SES rule set instead of creating per-branch\ncfe624c feat: auto-activate SES receipt rule set on deploy via custom resource\n040d64c fix: remove hardcoded SES rule set name to avoid cross-branch conflict\nec9794a fix: widen Bedrock IAM to all resources - inference profiles route cross-region\n1e16c83 fix: use AU inference profile for Haiku 4.5 + add inference-profile IAM permission\n44909f9 fix: switch to Claude Haiku 4.5 (3 Haiku is legacy)\n9dc8548 fix: correct GSI name to companyProfilesByExpenseIngestKey (Amplify Gen 2 naming)\n1b790fd fix: auto-add pending whitelist email on save\nff390c5 fix: handle nullable array entries when loading whitelisted emails from Amplify\n782467f fix: resolve CloudFormation circular dependency - move function and infra to data stack\nb7a824c feat: expense email ingest with sender whitelist (#1)\n",
                      "stderr": ""
                    }
                  }
                ],
                "status": "Success"
              }
            ]
          }
        },
        "timestamp": null,
        "images": null
      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "920db651-bce9-4c09-9d26-de5facd022a4",
          "content": "",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_eLAPZO4fWDyMcjfOIdBJAN",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "cd /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice && curl -s -X POST \\\n  -H \"Authorization: Bearer $(git credential fill <<< $'protocol=https\\nhost=github.com' 2>/dev/null | grep password | cut -d= -f2)\" \\\n  -H \"Accept: application/vnd.github+json\" \\\n  https://api.github.com/repos/DonthineniSagar/cloudpro-invoice/pulls \\\n  -d '{\n  \"title\": \"release: expense email ingest + fixes to prod\",\n  \"head\": \"main\",\n  \"base\": \"prod\",\n  \"body\": \"## Release to Production\\n\\n### Features\\n- Expense email ingest with sender whitelist\\n- Auto-create expenses from forwarded bills/receipts\\n\\n### Fixes since last prod merge\\n- CloudFormation circular dependency resolved\\n- Amplify nullable array loading for whitelisted emails\\n- Auto-add pending whitelist email on save\\n- GSI name corrected to Amplify Gen 2 convention\\n- Claude Haiku 4.5 with AU inference profile\\n- Bedrock IAM widened for cross-region inference profiles\\n- Shared SES rule set (no per-branch conflicts)\"\n}' 2>&1 | grep -E '\"number\"|\"html_url\"' | head -2",
                "summary": "Create PR from main to prod"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "cd /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice && curl -s -X POST \\\n  -H \"Authorization: Bearer $(git credential fill <<< $'protocol=https\\nhost=github.com' 2>/dev/null | grep password | cut -d= -f2)\" \\\n  -H \"Accept: application/vnd.github+json\" \\\n  https://api.github.com/repos/DonthineniSagar/cloudpro-invoice/pulls \\\n  -d '{\n  \"title\": \"release: expense email ingest + fixes to prod\",\n  \"head\": \"main\",\n  \"base\": \"prod\",\n  \"body\": \"## Release to Production\\n\\n### Features\\n- Expense email ingest with sender whitelist\\n- Auto-create expenses from forwarded bills/receipts\\n\\n### Fixes since last prod merge\\n- CloudFormation circular dependency resolved\\n- Amplify nullable array loading for whitelisted emails\\n- Auto-add pending whitelist email on save\\n- GSI name corrected to Amplify Gen 2 convention\\n- Claude Haiku 4.5 with AU inference profile\\n- Bedrock IAM widened for cross-region inference profiles\\n- Shared SES rule set (no per-branch conflicts)\"\n}' 2>&1 | grep -E '\"number\"|\"html_url\"' | head -2",
                "summary": "Create PR from main to prod"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      },

          "message_id": "838d3a31-9786-42d7-9197-df3c8695b604",
          "content": "The Lambda is being invoked (good — SES rule set is working) but Bedrock model access for Claude Haiku 4.5 hasn't been enabled. You need to enable it in the Bedrock console:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_ugbQFIqIBZ7v3yxTvZZQaU",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws bedrock get-foundation-model-availability --model-id anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0 2>&1",
                "summary": "Check if Haiku 4.5 model access is enabled"
              },

                    "Json": {
                      "exit_status": "0",
                      "stdout": "--------------------------------------------------------\n|                 ListFoundationModels                 |\n+--------------------------------------------+---------+\n|                     id                     | status  |\n+--------------------------------------------+---------+\n|  anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0  |  ACTIVE |\n+--------------------------------------------+---------+\n",

      "assistant": {
        "Response": {
          "message_id": "e1efd801-6398-46e5-be52-6f22dfa910b7",
          "content": "The model exists and is active, but the error says the Lambda's IAM role needs **Marketplace permissions** to subscribe to the model. This is a one-time model access enablement. You need to go to:\n\n**Bedrock console → Model access → Enable `Claude Haiku 4.5`**\n\nURL: https://ap-southeast-2.console.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/home?region=ap-southeast-2#/modelaccess\n\nClick \"Manage model access\" → tick `Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5` → Save. It takes about 1-2 minutes to activate.\n\nThe main branch worked because it was already enabled there (probably from a previous session). This is an account-level setting so once enabled it'll work for both branches.\n\nOnce you've done that, resend the email and it should go through."
        }
      },

        "content": {
          "Prompt": {
            "prompt": "it worked in same account with dev stack"

        },
        "timestamp": "2026-04-01T20:34:04.473679+13:00",

      },
      "assistant": {
        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "160f8023-1d13-4686-b4c3-3b379369576d",
          "content": "\n\nYou're right — same account, so model access should be the same. The error message is misleading. Let me look more carefully at the error — it mentions `aws-marketplace:ViewSubscriptions, aws-marketplace:Subscribe`. This is about the **Lambda's IAM role** not having marketplace permissions, not the account-level model access.\n\nThe main branch Lambda role might have different permissions. Let me check both:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_mcyrIjbrCwhBIOIvKo1GX1",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && for role in \"amplify-d1tnysmm95p3te-ma-processexpenseemaillambda-dbPQgchIb7s9\" \"amplify-d1tnysmm95p3te-pr-processexpenseemaillambda-ikL9htl40PbA\"; do echo \"=== $role ===\" && aws iam list-attached-role-policies --role-name \"$role\" --query 'AttachedPolicies[].PolicyName' --output text 2>&1 && aws iam list-role-policies --role-name \"$role\" --query 'PolicyNames' --output text 2>&1; done",
                "summary": "Compare IAM policies between main and prod Lambda roles"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && for role in \"amplify-d1tnysmm95p3te-ma-processexpenseemaillambda-dbPQgchIb7s9\" \"amplify-d1tnysmm95p3te-pr-processexpenseemaillambda-ikL9htl40PbA\"; do echo \"=== $role ===\" && aws iam list-attached-role-policies --role-name \"$role\" --query 'AttachedPolicies[].PolicyName' --output text 2>&1 && aws iam list-role-policies --role-name \"$role\" --query 'PolicyNames' --output text 2>&1; done",
                "summary": "Compare IAM policies between main and prod Lambda roles"
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      },

        "ToolUse": {
          "message_id": "37cccaef-02ed-4da8-b8bc-03cb88f0f604",
          "content": "IAM is identical — `bedrock:InvokeModel` on `*`. The permissions are fine. The error is actually about **Marketplace subscription** for the model. Claude Haiku 4.5 might need explicit model access enablement even though it shows as ACTIVE in the model list.\n\nLet me check:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_dfT5UG6QY71bru0wvdJAMz",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws bedrock get-foundation-model --model-identifier anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0 --query '{id:modelDetails.modelId,status:modelDetails.modelLifecycle.status,provider:modelDetails.providerName}' 2>&1",
                "summary": "Check model details"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws bedrock get-foundation-model --model-identifier anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0 --query '{id:modelDetails.modelId,status:modelDetails.modelLifecycle.status,provider:modelDetails.providerName}' 2>&1",
                "summary": "Check model details"
              }
            }
          ]
        }

          "content": "The model is active but the Marketplace subscription might not be complete. The thing is — main worked fine with the same model. The difference might be that the SES rule is routing to the **main** Lambda, not the prod one. Let me check:",
          "tool_uses": [
            {
              "id": "tooluse_AXaEiZisN3BCbathbqcyKZ",
              "name": "execute_bash",
              "orig_name": "execute_bash",
              "args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws ses describe-active-receipt-rule-set --query 'Rules[*].{Name:Name,Actions:Actions}' 2>&1",
                "summary": "Check which Lambda the SES rule is pointing to"
              },
              "orig_args": {
                "command": "source /Users/sdonthineni/projects/cloudpro/cloudpro-invoice/.env.creds && export AWS_REGION=ap-southeast-2 && aws ses describe-active-receipt-rule-set --query 'Rules[*].{Name:Name,Actions:Actions}' 2>&1",
                "summary": "Check which Lambda the SES rule is pointing to"
